body:
## Summary

Smoke-tested the README's 60-second quickstart against a fresh `pip install trellis-ai==0.5.2` from PyPI in a clean venv. Several steps failed for a brand-new user. This bundles the fixes for that path.

## What was broken

| # | Symptom | Root cause |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `trellis admin serve` crashed on a clean install with `RegistryValidationError: numpy is required for SQLiteVectorStore` | numpy lived in the `[vectors]` extra, but `SQLiteVectorStore` is the *default* local backend — and `_registry.validate()` runs at every server startup |
| 2 | `trellis retrieve entity user-api` returned `Entity not found` even though demo *did* seed a service called `user-api` | demo loader never populated `entity_aliases`; `retrieve entity` only did exact-id lookup |
| 3 | `trellis retrieve search 'user-api'` and `retrieve pack ...` printed bare ULIDs with no titles or content preview | the CLI looked for a non-existent `snippet` field on document-store search results |
| 4 | User-facing banner read `Starting XPG API server` | leftover from an old project rename |
| 5 | README + several docstrings referenced `~/.config/trellis/config.yaml` | actual default is `~/.trellis/config.yaml` (in both `trellis_cli/config.py:15` and `stores/registry.py:717`) |

## What this PR does

- **Promote `numpy>=1.24` to a core dep** in `pyproject.toml` (and remove the now-redundant entry from `[vectors]`).
- **Demo loader writes a `source_system="local"` alias** for every entity, mapping the human label to the ULID.
- **`retrieve entity` falls back to local-alias resolution** when exact-id lookup misses — so `retrieve entity user-api` resolves on demo data and on any user data that follows the same convention.
- **`retrieve search` / `retrieve pack` show a newline-collapsed content preview** instead of empty `snippet` fields.
- **Banner and library docstrings**: `XPG → Trellis` in `admin.py` (banner + `--help`), `config.py`, `registry.py`, `llm/__init__.py`. Design-doc / examples sweep is deliberately deferred — this PR sticks to user-visible strings + library docstrings.
- **Config-dir path corrected** in `README.md`, `scripts/seed_demo.py`, `mcp/server.py`, `plugins/__init__.py`, `retrieve/budget_config.py`, and `trellis_cli/admin.py` (3 spots).
- **Adds `test_entity_resolves_via_local_alias`** in `tests/unit/cli/test_retrieve.py` so the README example doesn't quietly re-break.

## Verified end-to-end

In a clean venv against this branch:

```
$ trellis admin init        ✓
$ trellis demo load         ✓ — 66 items, no deprecation warnings
$ trellis admin serve       ✓ — banner reads "Starting Trellis API server", UI 200
$ trellis retrieve entity user-api      ✓ — resolves to the service node + properties
$ trellis retrieve search 'user-api'    ✓ — six readable results with previews
$ trellis retrieve pack --intent ...    ✓ — readable previews
$ trellis retrieve traces --domain backend   ✓
```

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/unit/cli/test_retrieve.py` — 11 passed including new alias test
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/cli/` — 178 passed
- [x] `mypy src/trellis_cli/retrieve.py src/trellis_cli/demo.py` — clean
- [x] `ruff check` + `ruff format --check` — clean
- [x] Manual smoke against the README quickstart in a fresh venv — all six commands work

## Out of scope (separate work)

- The 22-file XPG sweep across design docs, examples, and TODO.md.
- CLI default log level (P1 #6 in the smoke-test report) — opinion-shaped, owner choice.
- Phase 6 PR 2 — still gated on v0.5.3 PyPI release per `adr-planes-and-substrates.md`.

Bumps [github/codeql-action](https://github.com/github/codeql-action) from 3 to 4.
Release notes

Sourced from github/codeql-action's releases.

v3.35.3

  • Upcoming breaking change: Add a deprecation warning for customers using CodeQL version 2.19.3 and earlier. These versions of CodeQL were discontinued on 9 April 2026 alongside GitHub Enterprise Server 3.15, and will be unsupported by the next minor release of the CodeQL Action. #3837
  • Configurations for private registries that use Cloudsmith or GCP OIDC are now accepted. #3850
  • Best-effort connection tests for private registries now use GET requests instead of HEAD for better compatibility with various registry implementations. For NuGet feeds, the test is now always performed against the service index. #3853
  • Fixed a bug where two diagnostics produced within the same millisecond could overwrite each other on disk, causing one of them to be lost. #3852
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.3. #3865

v3.35.2

  • The undocumented TRAP cache cleanup feature that could be enabled using the CODEQL_ACTION_CLEANUP_TRAP_CACHES environment variable is deprecated and will be removed in May 2026. If you are affected by this, we recommend disabling TRAP caching by passing the trap-caching: false input to the init Action. #3795
  • The Git version 2.36.0 requirement for improved incremental analysis now only applies to repositories that contain submodules. #3789
  • Python analysis on GHES no longer extracts the standard library, relying instead on models of the standard library. This should result in significantly faster extraction and analysis times, while the effect on alerts should be minimal. #3794
  • Fixed a bug in the validation of OIDC configurations for private registries that was added in CodeQL Action 4.33.0 / 3.33.0. #3807
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.2. #3823

v3.35.1

  • Fix incorrect minimum required Git version for improved incremental analysis: it should have been 2.36.0, not 2.11.0. #3781

v3.35.0

  • Reduced the minimum Git version required for improved incremental analysis from 2.38.0 to 2.11.0. #3767
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.1. #3773

v3.34.1

  • Downgrade default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.3 due to issues with a small percentage of Actions and JavaScript analyses. #3762

v3.34.0

  • Added an experimental change which disables TRAP caching when improved incremental analysis is enabled, since improved incremental analysis supersedes TRAP caching. This will improve performance and reduce Actions cache usage. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3569
  • We are rolling out improved incremental analysis to C/C++ analyses that use build mode none. We expect this rollout to be complete by the end of April 2026. #3584
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.0. #3585

v3.33.0

  • Upcoming change: Starting April 2026, the CodeQL Action will skip collecting file coverage information on pull requests to improve analysis performance. File coverage information will still be computed on non-PR analyses. Pull request analyses will log a warning about this upcoming change. #3562 To opt out of this change:
    • Repositories owned by an organization: Create a custom repository property with the name github-codeql-file-coverage-on-prs and the type "True/false", then set this property to true in the repository's settings. For more information, see Managing custom properties for repositories in your organization. Alternatively, if you are using an advanced setup workflow, you can set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
    • User-owned repositories using default setup: Switch to an advanced setup workflow and set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
    • User-owned repositories using advanced setup: Set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
  • Fixed a bug which caused the CodeQL Action to fail loading repository properties if a "Multi select" repository property was configured for the repository. #3557
  • The CodeQL Action now loads custom repository properties on GitHub Enterprise Server, enabling the customization of features such as github-codeql-disable-overlay that was previously only available on GitHub.com. #3559
  • Once private package registries can be configured with OIDC-based authentication for organizations, the CodeQL Action will now be able to accept such configurations. #3563
  • Fixed the retry mechanism for database uploads. Previously this would fail with the error "Response body object should not be disturbed or locked". #3564
  • A warning is now emitted if the CodeQL Action detects a repository property whose name suggests that it relates to the CodeQL Action, but which is not one of the properties recognised by the current version of the CodeQL Action. #3570

v3.32.6

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.3. #3548

v3.32.5

  • Repositories owned by an organization can now set up the github-codeql-disable-overlay custom repository property to disable improved incremental analysis for CodeQL. First, create a custom repository property with the name github-codeql-disable-overlay and the type "True/false" in the organization's settings. Then in the repository's settings, set this property to true to disable improved incremental analysis. For more information, see Managing custom properties for repositories in your organization. This feature is not yet available on GitHub Enterprise Server. #3507
  • Added an experimental change so that when improved incremental analysis fails on a runner — potentially due to insufficient disk space — the failure is recorded in the Actions cache so that subsequent runs will automatically skip improved incremental analysis until something changes (e.g. a larger runner is provisioned or a new CodeQL version is released). We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3487
  • The minimum memory check for improved incremental analysis is now skipped for CodeQL 2.24.3 and later, which has reduced peak RAM usage. #3515
  • Reduced log levels for best-effort private package registry connection check failures to reduce noise from workflow annotations. #3516
  • Added an experimental change which lowers the minimum disk space requirement for improved incremental analysis, enabling it to run on standard GitHub Actions runners. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3498

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from github/codeql-action's changelog.

4.32.3 - 13 Feb 2026

  • Added experimental support for testing connections to private package registries. This feature is not currently enabled for any analysis. In the future, it may be enabled by default for Default Setup. #3466

4.32.2 - 05 Feb 2026

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.1. #3460

4.32.1 - 02 Feb 2026

  • A warning is now shown in Default Setup workflow logs if a private package registry is configured using a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT), but no username is configured. #3422
  • Fixed a bug which caused the CodeQL Action to fail when repository properties cannot successfully be retrieved. #3421

4.32.0 - 26 Jan 2026

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.0. #3425

4.31.11 - 23 Jan 2026

  • When running a Default Setup workflow with Actions debugging enabled, the CodeQL Action will now use more unique names when uploading logs from the Dependabot authentication proxy as workflow artifacts. This ensures that the artifact names do not clash between multiple jobs in a build matrix. #3409
  • Improved error handling throughout the CodeQL Action. #3415
  • Added experimental support for automatically excluding generated files from the analysis. This feature is not currently enabled for any analysis. In the future, it may be enabled by default for some GitHub-managed analyses. #3318
  • The changelog extracts that are included with releases of the CodeQL Action are now shorter to avoid duplicated information from appearing in Dependabot PRs. #3403

4.31.10 - 12 Jan 2026

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.23.9. #3393

4.31.6 - 01 Dec 2025

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.23.6. #3321

4.31.4 - 18 Nov 2025

## Summary

Three small repo-hygiene chores bundled. Net **+113 / -44** across 3 files.

### 1. CodeQL security scanning (\`.github/workflows/codeql.yml\`)

GitHub-native security scanning, missing from the workflow set. Runs the \`security-extended\` query suite on push, PR, and a **weekly Monday 06:00 UTC cron** — the cron catches advisories that landed since the last push without waiting for new activity. Granted only the minimum permissions the action needs (\`actions: read\`, \`contents: read\`, \`security-events: write\`). Closes [TODO.md:2044](TODO.md:2044).

### 2. Dependabot config (\`.github/dependabot.yml\`)

Weekly Monday updates for two ecosystems:
- **\`pip\`** — Python dependencies in \`pyproject.toml\`
- **\`github-actions\`** — pinned action versions across all workflows

Minor + patch updates **grouped per ecosystem** into a single PR per week to avoid PR spam; major updates still surface individually since they need human review. \`open-pull-requests-limit: 5\` per ecosystem so churn stays bounded if updates pile up.

### 3. TODO.md sweep

The \`Repository Polish & Credibility Signals\` section was written before most of the work landed and was dramatically out of date. Flips ~14 entries from \`[ ]\` to \`[x]\`, each verified against the current repo state:

| Entry | What I checked |
|---|---|
| CI / Lint / Type Check / Tests badges | Already in \`README.md:7-12\` |
| Workflow split | \`lint.yml\`, \`tests.yml\`, \`typecheck.yml\` already exist; never had a monolithic \`ci.yml\` |
| PyPI / Python versions / License badges | Already in \`README.md:10-12\` |
| Database integration test workflow | \`live-infra.yml\` runs Neo4j + Postgres + pgvector live |
| Release workflow | \`publish.yml\` triggers on \`v*\` tags via OIDC |
| CONTRIBUTING / CODE_OF_CONDUCT / SECURITY / CHANGELOG | All exist at repo root |
| \`py.typed\` markers | All six packages |
| Makefile \`make check\` target | Aggregates lint + typecheck + test |
| GitHub topics | 17 topics covering the proposed set |
| CodeQL scanning | This PR |

The \`Ordering and dependencies\` subsection at the bottom now uses explicit ✅/⏳ markers per line so the next reader can see at a glance what's left in each phase.

### GitHub topics (out-of-band, externally visible)

Already done before opening this PR: \`gh repo edit --add-topic temporal-graph --add-topic llm-tools\` (17 topics total). Skipped \`llm-memory\` (redundant with \`agent-memory\`) and \`rag\` (redundant with \`retrieval-augmented-generation\`).

## Test plan

- [x] \`yaml.safe_load()\` on both new YAML files — clean
- [x] Local \`gh repo view\` confirms 17 topics post-edit
- [ ] CodeQL workflow runs on this PR (first check shows up after merge to main; can also dispatch manually post-merge to validate)
- [ ] Dependabot picks up the config on next scan (visible at Settings → Security & analysis → Dependabot)

## Genuinely still pending in the polish section

- GitHub Discussions enable (repo settings only, no code)
- Hero tagline tightening (judgement call; deferred for a separate prose-focused PR)
- Coverage measurement + Codecov badge (gated on ≥80% coverage target)
- README "How does this compare to X?" FAQ (substantive content, not polish)
- Quickstart smoke-test from clean venv (separate session worth)
- Demo GIF
- mkdocs-material docs site
- awesome-* list submissions

body:
## Summary

Phase 6 step 1 of [`adr-planes-and-substrates.md` §317-322](docs/design/adr-planes-and-substrates.md). All in-tree code now uses the plane-namespaced form ahead of the v0.6.0 alias removal. External callers see **no behavior change** — the flat aliases still exist and still emit \`DeprecationWarning\`.

This is the first half of a two-PR landing: once v0.5.3 ships with the deprecation entry visible in the CHANGELOG, PR 2 deletes the alias machinery, the flat-config branch, the \`TRELLIS_PG_DSN\` fallback, and the deprecation-asserting tests.

## What changed

**Source migrations (84 substitutions across 15 files)** — pure mechanical:

- \`registry.event_log\` → \`registry.operational.event_log\`
- \`registry.trace_store\` → \`registry.operational.trace_store\`
- \`registry.graph_store\` → \`registry.knowledge.graph_store\`
- \`registry.vector_store\` → \`registry.knowledge.vector_store\`
- \`registry.document_store\` → \`registry.knowledge.document_store\`
- \`registry.blob_store\` → \`registry.knowledge.blob_store\`

Coverage includes 13 \`src/\` files (78 sites), the \`scripts/seed_demo.py\` entry point (4 sites), and the \`examples/integrations/obsidian/README.md\` reference template (2 sites).

**CHANGELOG**: new \`### Deprecated\` section under \`[Unreleased]\` documenting the **v0.6.0 sunset target** for the flat aliases, the flat \`stores:\` config block, and the \`TRELLIS_PG_DSN\` env-var fallback. The warnings have been live since v0.4.0 but the sunset date was never published — this PR fixes that public-messaging gap.

## What's intentionally **not** in this PR

- The flat alias \`@property\` definitions, \`_warn_flat_property\` helper, \`_FLAT_PROPERTY_WARNED\` guard set, and \`_reset_deprecation_guards\` shim — all stay until PR 2.
- ~38 test-side flat-property accesses across 5 files (\`test_handlers.py\`, \`test_server.py\`, \`test_analyze.py\`, \`test_registry_planes.py\`, \`test_registry_integration.py\`) — these intentionally exercise the deprecation path. Pytest reports only **3 distinct \`DeprecationWarning\`s** because \`_FLAT_PROPERTY_WARNED\` is a one-shot guard set per property name. PR 2 converts/deletes all ~38 sites alongside removing the aliases.
- The flat \`stores:\` config branch in \`_extract_store_config\` and the \`TRELLIS_PG_DSN\` fallback in DSN resolution — same release cycle, deleted in PR 2.

## Test plan

The ADR promised a "one-release deprecation window," and four releases (v0.4.0 → v0.5.2) have included the warnings. But [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) had no \`### Deprecated\` section and no sunset date — external consumers who upgraded through v0.3.x–v0.5.x had been told *something is deprecated* but never *when it goes away*. Removing the aliases now in a single PR risked surprising any external installs with a hard \`AttributeError\`. Splitting into "internal call-site migration + public sunset notice" (this PR, ships in v0.5.3) and "machinery removal" (next PR, ships in v0.6.0) closes that gap with one extra release tag of cost.

## Summary

Closes the *standardize all curate paths together* deferral that landed in commit `bbc675a` ("the existing convention wins until we standardize all curate paths together"). Every site that submits a Command / CommandBatch through the governed mutation pipeline now stamps a meaningful audit-trail identifier rather than the generic `"cli"` / `"api"` / `"save_memory_extractor"` it used to.

## Convention

`<surface>:<verb>` — matches the existing `cli:dbt-manifest` / `cli:openlineage` precedent already in `trellis_cli.ingest`. Documented in the `Command` and `CommandBatch` docstrings.

| Surface | Examples |
|---|---|
| `cli` | `cli:link`, `cli:label`, `cli:entity`, `cli:feedback`, `cli:promote-learning`, `cli:promote` |
| `api` (hardcoded routes) | `api:link`, `api:entity`, `api:feedback` |
| `api` (wire DTO defaults — used when client omits `requested_by`) | `api:promote`, `api:mutations`, `api:bulk-ingest` |
| `mcp` | `mcp:save_memory` |

`Command.requested_by` keeps `"unknown"` as a safety fallback — it should never appear in production events because every call site stamps its own identity now.

## Why

Audit-side: operators can grep events by surface (`requested_by LIKE 'mcp:%'`) or by verb (`LIKE '%:link'`). The old generic `"cli"` / `"api"` strings carried no information beyond "something happened on this transport."

## Summary

Fills the gap left after #91 — the operator runbook frames production as self-hosted Neo4j in the cloud, but the only deployment guides shipped were ``neo4j-local.md`` (laptop Docker) and ``neo4j-auradb.md`` (managed). Operators reading the runbook cold were on their own for the actual production target.

New ``docs/deployment/neo4j-self-hosted-cloud.md`` covers what's specific to running your own Neo4j in your own cloud — the things the laptop + AuraDB docs deliberately don't.

## What it covers

| Section | Why it's here vs the existing docs |
|---|---|
| Edition decision (Community vs Enterprise) | Tied directly to Trellis's multi-writer SCD-2 needs — Community = single writer only; Enterprise = `NODE KEY` constraint for safe concurrent writers |
| Hosting target table (Helm / Docker-VM / bare EC2) | The AuraDB doc is managed-only; this is the choice operators face |
| Bolt exposure (in-cluster Service DNS vs cross-VPC TLS via `neo4j+s://`) | Networking is operator-side here, not Aura-side |
| `TRELLIS_NEO4J_USER` / `DATABASE` always `neo4j` | Self-hosted has no AuraDB-Free instance-ID conflation; called out as a contrast |
| Backup matrix | Community = offline `neo4j-admin database dump`; Enterprise = online backup |
| Driver pool sizing for self-hosted concurrency | Different from AuraDB-Pro-class defaults |
| Prometheus / log scraping | Enterprise exposes `:2004/metrics`; Community is JMX-only |
| Troubleshooting | NetworkPolicy blocks, cert chain issues, Causal Cluster lag, restored-with-different-DB-name |

## Cross-links updated

- Runbook intro now lists all 5 deployment guides
- ``neo4j-local.md`` and ``neo4j-auradb.md`` "Next steps" sections point at the new doc

## Design choice

The doc deliberately points at Neo4j's own resources (Helm chart repo, persistent-volume guide) for the deep dives rather than duplicating them. Trellis's job is to document the integration, not re-explain Neo4j operations.

- [x] `pytest` — n/a (docs only)
- [x] All cross-link anchors verified by hand against actual headings (em-dash → double-dash conversion)

## Summary

Four pieces of POC deploy hardening, batched because they're tightly coupled — the runbook documents the deploy surface, the smoke-test command lets operators validate a deploy in one shot, the CI job protects the surface from regressions, and the eval fix is the real-world example of the regression class the CI is meant to catch.

- **Operator runbook** at `docs/deployment/runbook.md` — single page covering env vars, probes, metrics, pool sizing, the three closed loops, and the reconciliation recovery path. Frames Neo4j generically (production target is self-hosted Neo4j in the cloud); AuraDB-specific quirks stay in the docs but as labelled exceptions, not defaults.
- **`trellis admin smoke-test` command** in `src/trellis_cli/admin.py` — operationalises the runbook's "Validating a deployment" checklist as a single command that exits 0 when every check passes and 1 on any failure. Five checks: `/healthz`, `/readyz` (with per-backend latency surfaced), auth-rejects-missing, auth-accepts-valid, `/metrics` (404 reported as INFO, not FAIL — observability is an opt-in extra). Catches the dangerous misconfig where `TRELLIS_API_KEY` is set in operator env but not loaded server-side. Suitable for k8s init-container hooks or deploy-pipeline gates. 12 tests using `httpx.MockTransport`; runbook updated to lead with the command.
- **Live-infra CI workflow** at `.github/workflows/live-infra.yml` — runs `tests/integration/test_neo4j_e2e.py` + `tests/integration/loops/` against a live Neo4j + Postgres on **push to main** (every merge runs the live suite, catching regressions when they actually land) and on **workflow_dispatch** (on-demand pre-deploy validation). No cron — live infra doesn't drift on its own. Concurrency-locked so simultaneous runs don't corrupt shared state via `_live_wipe`.
- **Live-infra regression fix** in `eval/_live_wipe.py` — PR-2 turned `PostgresStoreBase._conn` into a context-manager method, but the eval truncate helper was still doing `getattr(store, "_conn")` and treating the bound method as a connection. Crashed all five `tests/integration/loops/` tests at fixture setup. Switched to `with store._conn() as conn`, dropped the now-redundant explicit commit, removed the dead defensive branch.

## Action required from reviewer

Five repo secrets to set under **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions** before merges to main do anything useful in CI:

- `TRELLIS_TEST_NEO4J_URI`, `TRELLIS_TEST_NEO4J_USER`, `TRELLIS_TEST_NEO4J_PASSWORD`, `TRELLIS_TEST_NEO4J_DATABASE`
- `TRELLIS_TEST_PG_DSN`

Without them the workflow runs but every test skips cleanly, so secret rollout can happen out-of-band from this PR.

- [x] `pytest tests/unit/cli/` — 177/177 pass; smoke-test addition doesn't regress siblings
- [x] Workflow YAML parses; triggers are `push` (main only) and `workflow_dispatch`, no cron
- [x] Runbook URLs match actual route mounts
- [ ] Reviewer sets the 5 GitHub Actions secrets so the next merge to main runs the live suite
- [ ] First post-merge run lands green
- [ ] Reviewer runs `trellis admin smoke-test` against an actual deploy (or `python -m trellis_cli.main admin smoke-test --url ...`) to confirm the runbook's claimed validation flow works in practice

## What this PR does *not* tackle

- Self-hosted Neo4j cloud deployment guide (gap in `docs/deployment/`; production target is self-hosted but only laptop + AuraDB guides exist today)
- Day-1-with-Trellis agent quickstart (operator side is now covered; agent on-ramp is the bigger remaining work for "POC running")

## Summary

POC hardening — **final PR in the series**. Closes the "no spans, no metrics, can't tell why a `/packs` call slowed down" gap.

### The new `observability` extra

Optional dependency group in [`pyproject.toml`](pyproject.toml):

- `opentelemetry-api` / `-sdk` / `-exporter-otlp` — core SDK + OTLP exporter (works with Honeycomb, Grafana Tempo, Datadog, …).
- `opentelemetry-instrumentation-fastapi` — per-request spans with method, route, status, latency.
- `opentelemetry-instrumentation-psycopg` — per-statement spans + connection metrics.
- `prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator` — `/metrics` endpoint with per-route latency histograms + counters.

`trellis-ai[all]` now pulls these in. Deployments that want to pick-and-choose still use explicit extras.

### The `observability.py` module

New [`src/trellis_api/observability.py`](src/trellis_api/observability.py) exposing `install_observability(app)`:

- **No-ops cleanly when the extra isn't installed** — each `try` / `except ImportError` returns False with a debug log.
- **No-ops when `TRELLIS_DISABLE_OBSERVABILITY` is set** — for deploys that pull `[all]` but want to opt out.
- Wires FastAPI auto-instrumentation, psycopg auto-instrumentation, and the Prometheus `/metrics` route.
- Logs `observability_installed` with which pieces wired up.

OTel exporter is no-op until `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set — install-but-don't-configure costs no telemetry quota. `/metrics` is deliberately unauthenticated for the same reason `/healthz` and `/readyz` are: orchestrator scrape jobs need it open.

## Summary

POC hardening — **eighth in the series**. Closes the "fetch megabytes of JSON to filter in Python" pattern flagged in the audit by giving `EventLog.get_events` a `payload_filters` kwarg that pushes key/value predicates into the backend SQL.

### The new kwarg

```python
event_log.get_events(payload_filters={"domain": "billing"})
```

`payload_filters` maps payload-key → expected string value; predicates are AND-ed and flow into:
- **Postgres**: `payload->>'k' = 'v'` (JSONB, GIN-indexable).
- **SQLite**: `json_extract(payload_json, '$.' || ?) = ?` with the key as a bound parameter (key isn't interpolated SQL — safe from injection).

Comparison is against the textual JSON value, so callers comparing ints / bools must coerce to `str`. Documented in the ABC docstring.

### Call sites updated

1. **`list_precedents`** ([retrieve/precedents.py](src/trellis/retrieve/precedents.py)) — `domain=` is now pushed into SQL so the `limit` cap applies *after* the filter. The previous post-fetch shape silently returned fewer than `limit` matches when more existed beyond the cap.
2. **`_feedback_id_in_event_log`** ([feedback/recording.py](src/trellis/feedback/recording.py)) — replaces the 10K-row scan + Python `any(...)` with a single-row predicate against `payload->>'feedback_id'`. Combined with the partial JSON-key index from PR-3 ([#86](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/86)), this lookup is now O(log N) per `record_feedback` call.

### Out of scope (deferred)

`effectiveness.py` and `advisory_generator.py` were also flagged in the audit but use a pack_id→payload dict-and-join pattern that doesn't fit `payload_filters` cleanly. Separate follow-up if profiling shows the auditor's truncation-pressure caveat (from PR [#82](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/82)) actually bites.

## Tests

- `test_event_log.py` (SQLite path) — 4 new tests: single key, multiple keys (AND), no match, empty/None default.
- `test_postgres_stores.py` (Postgres path, gated by `TRELLIS_TEST_PG_DSN`) — 3 mirror tests for the JSONB path.
- `test_feedback.py` — regression for `_feedback_id_in_event_log` (returns False for unknown id, True for known).
- `test_precedents.py` (new file) — 4 tests including the limit-after-filter regression: 20 non-matching + 5 matching rows, asserts `limit=3` with `domain=` returns 3 matching (not 0).

## Summary

POC hardening — **seventh in the series** after [#83](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/83), [#84](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/84), [#85](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/85), [#86](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/86), [#87](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/87). Two unrelated reliability fixes, bundled because they're both leftover ops gaps from the audit.

### 1. MCP server graceful shutdown

The previous `main()` in [`src/trellis/mcp/server.py`](src/trellis/mcp/server.py) called `mcp.run()` and never closed the cached `StoreRegistry` it constructed. Postgres connection pool + Neo4j driver leaked until process death.

- `main()` now wraps `mcp.run()` in `try` / `finally` that calls `_registry.close()` on exit (clean or via exception).
- `_install_shutdown_signal_handlers()` registers best-effort handlers for SIGTERM / SIGINT. SIGINT re-raises `KeyboardInterrupt` so `mcp.run()` unwinds; SIGTERM is swallowed (the parent typically follows with stdin EOF). Handler does NOT call `sys.exit()` — that can corrupt stdout mid-write.
- Platform-specific `ValueError` / `OSError` / `AttributeError` on `signal.signal` are caught — Windows doesn't deliver SIGTERM to Python the same way POSIX does.

### 2. Subgraph depth cap

`GraphStore.get_subgraph(depth=N)` had no upper bound. With `depth=10` on a 1M-node graph at average degree 5, the recursive CTE / variable-length-path query explores ~10M paths before edge-type filtering — DoS-class slow.

- New `MAX_SUBGRAPH_DEPTH` constant in [`src/trellis/stores/base/graph.py`](src/trellis/stores/base/graph.py), defaulting to 6, tunable via `TRELLIS_GRAPH_MAX_SUBGRAPH_DEPTH`. Invalid env values fall back to the default with a logged warning.
- New `validate_subgraph_depth()` helper rejects negative depths AND depths above the cap with a message pointing at the env var.
- All three `get_subgraph` implementations call `validate_subgraph_depth(depth)` at the top before any backend work. Removed the duplicate `depth < 0` check from Neo4j.

## Tests

- [`TestMainShutdown`](tests/unit/mcp/test_server.py) — 3 cases: registry closed on clean exit, registry closed when `mcp.run` raises, no-op when no registry was constructed.
- [`test_subgraph_rejects_depth_above_cap`](tests/unit/stores/contracts/graph_store_contract.py) — single contract test runs against all 3 backends.

- [x] `ruff check` on touched files: clean
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/mcp/ tests/unit/stores/contracts/` — 190 passed, 76 skipped

## Summary

POC hardening — **fifth in the series** after [#83](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/83), [#84](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/84), [#85](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/85). Three pieces of FastAPI plumbing bundled because they all touch the same middleware + exception layer.

### 1. Request-ID correlation

[`src/trellis_api/middleware.py`](src/trellis_api/middleware.py) — `request_id_middleware`:
- Reads `X-Request-ID` from the inbound request, or mints a fresh ULID.
- Binds it via `structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars` so every log line for the request carries the ID.
- Sets it on `request.state.request_id` for route handlers.
- Echoes it back as a response header.

Closes the "agent says X happened but logs show Y" debugging gap.

### 2. Real `/readyz` backend probes

[`routes/health.py`](src/trellis_api/routes/health.py) — the old probe only counted operational EventLog rows, so an AuraDB or pgvector outage left `/readyz` returning 200 while every `/api/v1/packs` call 500'd. Now probes 4 backends:

| Backend | Probe |
|---|---|
| operational `event_log` | `count()` |
| knowledge `graph_store` | `count_nodes()` |
| knowledge `vector_store` | `count()` |
| knowledge `document_store` | `count()` |

Response body breakdown so operators see which backend is down without grepping logs:

```json
{
  "status": "degraded",
  "backends": {
    "event_log": {"status": "ok", "latency_ms": 1.23},
    "vector_store": {"status": "degraded", "latency_ms": 89.4, "error": "connection refused"},
    ...
  }
}
```

Any single failure flips overall status to **503**.

### 3. Structured 500 envelope

`unhandled_exception_handler` translates uncaught `Exception` to `{"code": "internal_error", "message": "internal server error", "request_id": ...}` with HTTP 500. The full exception is logged via `logger.exception` with `request_id` bound — operators get the traceback in the structured log stream; clients see no internal types or schema info. `HTTPException` deliberately passes through unchanged (it's FastAPI's signal, not a leak).

## Tests

- [`test_middleware.py`](tests/unit/api/test_middleware.py) — 6 new tests: ID generation / propagation / uniqueness, 500 envelope shape + request_id correlation, HTTPException pass-through.
- [`test_health.py`](tests/unit/api/test_health.py) — strengthened to assert all 4 backends in the response body + a new `test_degraded_when_a_backend_throws` case using `monkeypatch` on the vector store.

## Test plan

## Summary

Adds four missing indices on the `events` table so the hot-path EventLog reads become index lookups instead of sequential scans on a multi-million-row table.

This is part of the POC-hardening series:
- PR-1 (#83) — security: remove `/self-update` RCE + default bind to 127.0.0.1
- PR-2 (#84) — connection pooling
- **PR-3 (this PR)** — missing EventLog indices
- (later) PR replacing the 10K-row scan in `_feedback_id_in_event_log` with a direct JSON-key lookup, now that the index exists

## What landed

All four indices added to the existing `_CREATE_INDEXES` list in `src/trellis/stores/postgres/event_log.py`. They use `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`, so existing deployments pick them up on next process start — no separate migration script needed.

| Index | Shape | Query it accelerates |
|---|---|---|
| `idx_events_type_occurred_desc` | `(event_type, occurred_at DESC)` | `get_events(event_type=X, order="desc", limit=N)` — no-sort index range scan |
| `idx_events_entity_occurred_desc` | `(entity_id, occurred_at DESC)` | Entity-history lookups |
| `idx_events_feedback_id` | partial btree on `(payload->>'feedback_id')` where `event_type = 'feedback.recorded'` | `_feedback_id_in_event_log` (every `record_feedback` call) — its 10K-row scan becomes O(log N) immediately |
| `idx_events_idempotency_key` | partial btree on `(payload->>'idempotency_key')` where `event_type = 'mutation.executed'` | `PostgresEventLog.has_idempotency_key` — every command in the mutation pipeline |

The previous single-column `idx_events_type` + `idx_events_occurred_at` did not serve the recency-ordered slice well — Postgres still needed a sort step. The composite removes the sort.

No callers or signatures changed — purely additive schema. SQLite is intentionally untouched (different partial-index syntax for JSON, and SQLite isn't the production target).

## Verification

Three tests added to `TestPostgresEventLog` in `tests/unit/stores/test_postgres_stores.py`. They're gated by the existing `TRELLIS_TEST_PG_DSN` skipif at the top of the file — local runs skip them, CI runs them against the live Postgres.

- `test_init_schema_is_idempotent` — calls `_init_schema()` twice in a row, asserts no error. Confirms `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS` works as advertised.
- `test_explain_uses_type_occurred_desc_index` — `EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM events WHERE event_type = 'feedback.recorded' ORDER BY occurred_at DESC LIMIT 10` and asserts the plan text contains `idx_events_type_occurred_desc`. This confirms the optimizer actually picks the new composite index for this query shape.
- `test_explain_uses_idempotency_key_index` — `EXPLAIN SELECT 1 FROM events WHERE event_type = 'mutation.executed' AND payload->>'idempotency_key' = 'k1' LIMIT 1` and asserts the plan text contains `idx_events_idempotency_key`. Confirms the partial JSON expression index is used for the idempotency check.

## Test plan

- [x] `uv run ruff check src/trellis/stores/postgres/event_log.py tests/unit/stores/test_postgres_stores.py` — clean
- [x] `uv run ruff format` — no changes
- [x] `uv run pytest tests/unit/ -x -q` — 2342 passed, 144 skipped (Postgres tests skip without DSN, as expected)
- [ ] CI runs the EXPLAIN tests against live Postgres

## Summary

POC hardening — **fourth in the series** after [#83](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/83), [#84](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/84).

### The gap

Every router under `/api/v1` was mounted with no authentication. Anyone reachable on the bind address (now `127.0.0.1` by default thanks to PR-1, but the moment a deployment opens it up to a pod IP, the API has zero auth) could POST to `/commands/batch`, `/ingest/bulk`, or any other mutating route.

### The fix

Added [`src/trellis_api/auth.py`](src/trellis_api/auth.py) with a `require_api_key` FastAPI dependency:

- **Env-gated**: reads `TRELLIS_API_KEY` from environment at request time (tests can monkeypatch).
- **Unset → no-op**: dev / CI workflows stay frictionless.
- **Set → require `X-API-Key` header to match**, using `secrets.compare_digest` for constant-time comparison.
- **401 on rejection** with body `{"detail": "missing or invalid X-API-Key"}` and `WWW-Authenticate: ApiKey`.

Wired via `dependencies=[Depends(require_api_key)]` on every `/api/v1` router include in [`app.py:create_app()`](src/trellis_api/app.py).

### What stays open (deliberately)

- `/healthz`, `/readyz` — orchestrator probes must work without holding the API secret.
- `/api/version` — clients need to probe major-version compatibility before they have a key.
- `/ui/` static files — auth is at the API layer; the UI's `fetch` calls carry the header per request.

### Startup warning

The FastAPI lifespan now calls `warn_if_unauthenticated()` which logs `api_key_unset` at WARNING level when the env var is missing — so a production boot surfaces the gap in the normal log stream rather than as a silent backdoor.

## Tests

10 new tests in [`tests/unit/api/test_auth.py`](tests/unit/api/test_auth.py):

- `TestAuthDisabledByDefault` (2 cases) — unset env = no auth.
- `TestAuthEnforced` (3 cases) — missing / wrong / correct header.
- `TestProbesStayOpen` (3 cases) — healthz / readyz / version stay open even when secret is set.
- `TestMutationsRoutesGated` (2 cases) — POST routes get the same gate as reads.

All 10 pass. Existing 74 API tests continue green (none of them set `TRELLIS_API_KEY`).

## Summary

POC hardening — **second of the series** after [#83](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/83). The biggest single performance fix in the hardening pass.

### The bug

Every Postgres-backed store ([`PostgresTraceStore`](src/trellis/stores/postgres/trace.py), [`PostgresDocumentStore`](src/trellis/stores/postgres/document.py), [`PostgresGraphStore`](src/trellis/stores/postgres/graph.py), [`PostgresEventLog`](src/trellis/stores/postgres/event_log.py), [`PgVectorStore`](src/trellis/stores/pgvector/store.py)) held **one** `psycopg.Connection` per process and routed every cursor through it. psycopg connections aren't safe for concurrent cursor use, so FastAPI's thread-pool handlers serialised through the single connection — under load the API blocked on itself even when Postgres had plenty of capacity.

### The fix

[`PostgresStoreBase`](src/trellis/stores/postgres/base.py) now owns a `psycopg_pool.ConnectionPool`. Subclasses acquire a connection via a new `_conn()` context manager that:
- checks out from the pool
- yields the connection
- commits on success / rolls back on exception
- returns the connection to the pool

Pool sizing is env-tunable: `TRELLIS_PG_POOL_MIN_SIZE` / `TRELLIS_PG_POOL_MAX_SIZE` (defaults 2 / 20). Ignored when the caller supplies a pre-built `pool=` kwarg.

### `PgVectorStore` refactored to inherit from `PostgresStoreBase`

Was the only Postgres-backed store managing its own connection — a duplicate of the base pattern. Now uses an `on_connect` callback to register the `pgvector` type adapter against each newly-opened pooled connection.

### Race-condition fix bundled in

The previous single-shared-connection model accidentally serialised all writes on the same store. The pool removes that implicit serialization. To preserve correctness:

- `upsert_node`, `upsert_edge`, `upsert_alias` now hold ONE pooled connection across the read+write so SCD-2 close-and-reinsert stays transactional.
- Single-row paths added `FOR UPDATE` row locks so concurrent upserts of the same row block instead of racing.
- `upsert_edges_bulk` and `compact_versions` similarly wrap their multi-statement work in one `_conn()`.

### Migration scope

~75 cursor sites across event_log / trace / document / graph / pgvector + 5 external callers (2 routes, 2 tests). Net diff: +583 / −387 across 10 files.

### Tests

- **`TestPostgresConnectionPool::test_concurrent_writes_do_not_deadlock`** — 8 threads × 25 writes, asserts no deadlock and final count matches.
- **`TestPostgresConnectionPool::test_pool_respects_max_size_env_var`** — confirms env-var plumbing.

Both gated behind `TRELLIS_TEST_PG_DSN` (existing skipif).

## Test plan

- [x] `ruff check` + `ruff format --check` clean

- [x] No remaining `self.conn` / `store.conn` references in `src/` or `tests/` outside SQLite stores

## Summary

POC hardening — **first of a series**. Two unrelated-but-bundled safety fixes; both must land together so a fresh deployment isn't simultaneously exposed and remotely-pin-able.

### 1. Remove `POST /api/v1/self-update`

[`trellis_api/routes/admin.py`](src/trellis_api/routes/admin.py) — endpoint shelled out to `pip install git+...{ref}` and `systemctl restart trellis-api` with **no authentication**. Anyone reachable on the bind address could pin the process to any commit on any fork (supply-chain RCE) or DoS via repeated restarts.

Audit confirmed no test, doc, script, or skill consumes this endpoint:

```
$ grep -r self_update src/ tests/ docs/
src/trellis_api/routes/admin.py     # only this file
```

Updates flow through the deployment pipeline now. If we ever bring this surface back it MUST be auth-gated and constrained to a signed allowlist of refs — but the cleanest path is to keep deploys out of the running process entirely.

### 2. Default bind to `127.0.0.1`

Previous default `0.0.0.0` exposed the unauthenticated REST API on every interface.

- [`trellis_api/app.py`](src/trellis_api/app.py) `DEFAULT_HOST` reads `TRELLIS_API_HOST` env var (default `127.0.0.1`); `DEFAULT_PORT` likewise reads `TRELLIS_API_PORT`.
- [`trellis_cli/serve.py`](src/trellis_cli/serve.py) and [`trellis_cli/admin.py`](src/trellis_cli/admin.py) `serve` command help text documents the env override.

Containers that need to listen on the pod IP set `TRELLIS_API_HOST=0.0.0.0` (or pass `--host 0.0.0.0`) explicitly. Loopback-only is the safer default until API key auth lands in **PR-4**.

Ordered by POC risk. PR-1 (this one) and PR-2 land sync; PR-3/6/7/8 will be parallel worktree agents once PR-2's foundation is in place.

## Summary

PR B from the [#78 simplify pass](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/78). Fixes a silent-correctness bug surfaced during /simplify, with full caller audit + targeted fixes.

### The bug

[`EventLog.get_events`](src/trellis/stores/base/event_log.py) sorted `ORDER BY occurred_at ASC LIMIT N` in both Postgres and SQLite backends. [`_feedback_id_in_event_log`](src/trellis/feedback/recording.py) at recording.py:73 claimed in its docstring to "scan the **most recent** FEEDBACK_RECORDED events" — but with `limit=10_000` on a busy log it actually scanned the *oldest* 10K. So a recent duplicate `feedback_id` could silently slip past the duplicate-check, causing:

- `record_feedback` to **double-emit** when the same feedback was replayed.
- `reconcile_feedback_log_to_event_log` to **break idempotency** — re-emitting recently-emitted feedback as if it were new.

### The fix

Add `order: Literal["asc", "desc"] = "asc"` to [`EventLog.get_events`](src/trellis/stores/base/event_log.py) (ABC + [Postgres](src/trellis/stores/postgres/event_log.py) + [SQLite](src/trellis/stores/sqlite/event_log.py)). Default stays ASC so existing analytics callers keep their current behavior unchanged — most notably [`evaluate.py:578`](src/trellis/retrieve/evaluate.py) which relies on ASC ordering for "latest feedback wins when duplicated" dict semantics.

### Callers fixed (the two B-verdicts from the audit)

1. **`_feedback_id_in_event_log`** ([recording.py](src/trellis/feedback/recording.py)) — duplicate-check + reconcile-already-present short-circuits. Now passes `order="desc"`. Docstring updated to match behavior.
2. **`list_precedents`** ([retrieve/precedents.py](src/trellis/retrieve/precedents.py)) — UI-facing precedent listing consumed by REST, CLI, and MCP. `limit=20` with no `since=` filter; under ASC it returned the oldest 20 and missed every recent promotion. Docstring now also flags the "post-filter on `domain` after truncation" caveat.

### Caller audit verdicts

Audited all 14 `get_events` call sites in `src/trellis/`. Verdicts:

- **B (needs DESC fix)**: 2 — both fixed above.
- **A (chronological / order-invariant)**: 11 — analytics aggregators (effectiveness, advisory_generator, observations, telemetry, evaluate, etc.) that use dicts/counters and either don't care about order or rely on ASC for last-write-wins.
- **C (bounded scan, set-union)**: 3 — set membership / dedup, order-irrelevant.

### Tests

- **[`test_event_log.py`](tests/unit/stores/test_event_log.py)** — 3 new tests covering `asc`/`desc` semantics and `limit` truncation in both directions.
- **[`TestFeedbackIdScanLimitRegression`](tests/unit/feedback/test_feedback.py)** — new regression seeding 10,050 older `FEEDBACK_RECORDED` events plus one target at the recent end; asserts `_feedback_id_in_event_log` finds the target. Under the bug this returned False.
- **`_CapturingEventLog`** test stub updated to honor `order=` so existing fake-backend tests continue to mirror real-backend semantics.

### Documentation

- [`docs/research/client-layer-inventory.md:303`](docs/research/client-layer-inventory.md) — `EventLog` row updated to include `limit` and `order` in the `get_events` signature.

## Out of scope (spawned as a follow-up task)

Several analytics aggregators ([effectiveness.py:270/277/618/623](src/trellis/retrieve/effectiveness.py), [advisory_generator.py:82/87](src/trellis/retrieve/advisory_generator.py), [learning/observations.py:78/83](src/trellis/learning/observations.py)) use `limit=1000`/`5000` with a `since=` window and the default ASC. Under truncation pressure they bias toward stale data. The mechanism is now in place; the per-caller audit + selective fix lands separately.

## Test plan

- [x] `ruff check` + `ruff format --check` clean
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/` — **2346 passed, 284 skipped** (was 2342; +4 new tests)
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/stores/test_event_log.py tests/unit/feedback/test_feedback.py` — 59 passed
- [x] `pytest -k precedent` — 3 passed (REST, CLI, schema)

## Summary

Adds `tests/integration/loops/test_reconciliation_loop.py` — proves the divergence-recovery primitive [`reconcile_feedback_log_to_event_log`](src/trellis/feedback/recording.py) heals end-to-end against live infra:

    seed corpus → pack 1 (distractor included) →
    write JSONL feedback ONLY (no event_log kwarg) →
    confirm EventLog has no matching FEEDBACK_RECORDED →
    reconcile_feedback_log_to_event_log →
    EventLog now has the events; rerun reconcile is a no-op →
    apply-noise-tags via REST → distractor tagged →
    pack 2 (distractor excluded by signal_quality filter)

Four invariants exercised in one walk of the loop:

1. **Divergence** — `record_feedback(event_log=None)` writes only to `pack_feedback.jsonl`; the EventLog stays empty for that `feedback_id`.
2. **Reconciliation** — `reconcile_feedback_log_to_event_log` emits the missing event with payload bridged via `PackFeedback.to_event_payload` (`helpful_item_ids` carry `items_referenced`).
3. **Idempotency** — rerunning reconcile is a no-op: `scanned=1, emitted=0, already_present=1`.
4. **Equivalence** — the reconciled `FEEDBACK_RECORDED` drives the same downstream analytics a live emission would have. `apply-noise-tags` flags the distractor; pack 2 excludes it via the default `signal_quality` allowlist.

## Notes

- The test writes JSONL via `record_feedback` from `trellis.feedback` directly — same code path production uses for file-only capture; the test deliberately skips the MCP `record_feedback` tool here so the EventLog stays empty until reconcile runs.
- Reuses the existing `seed_distractor_corpus` / `build_pack` / `trigger_apply_noise_tags` helpers from `tests/integration/loops/conftest.py` (mirrors the noise-demote loop's structure with the JSONL→reconcile divergence step inserted between seed and apply-noise-tags).
- Reconciler opens its own `StoreRegistry` (scoped DSN env vars) so it reads / writes the same Postgres EventLog the spawned uvicorn uses.
- Skipped when `TRELLIS_TEST_NEO4J_URI` or `TRELLIS_TEST_PG_DSN` is unset, matching the rest of the loop suite.

## Followup (separate PR)

The `_live_registry` context manager helper is duplicated between #77 (H2.3) and this PR. Once both land, a tiny refactor PR can pull it into `tests/integration/loops/conftest.py` so future loop tests don't need to re-paste the env-var dance.

## Summary

Closes the EventLog-authoritative promote half of the dual-loop end to end at the public surface, and adds the **operator CLI surface** the H2.3 hand-off note flagged as missing.

### Operator surface (new)

Two new CLI commands wrap the pure functions in `trellis.learning` and route mutations through the governed pipeline:

```
trellis analyze learning-candidates --output-dir <path> [--days N] [--min-support N]
  → Read-only. Scans the EventLog, scores observations, writes
    intent_learning_candidates.json + promotion_decisions.template.json
    into --output-dir for human review.

trellis curate promote-learning --candidates <path> --decisions <path> [--dry-run]
  → Reads filled-in decisions, runs prepare_learning_promotions to build
    entity + edge payloads, and submits approved promotions through
    MutationExecutor (ENTITY_CREATE + per-target LINK_CREATE).
    --dry-run previews payloads without committing.
```

Plus a production fix in [src/trellis_cli/stores.py](src/trellis_cli/stores.py): `_get_registry` now uses `StoreRegistry.from_config_dir` instead of `TrellisConfig.load`. The previous path silently rejected plane-split YAML (`TrellisConfig` forbids extra fields per project convention), so the CLI surface was unusable in any cloud deployment. The new path accepts both flat (legacy) and plane-split (cloud) shapes — the registry already supports both.

### End-to-end loop test

[tests/integration/loops/test_learning_promote_loop.py](tests/integration/loops/test_learning_promote_loop.py) drives the full loop through public surfaces:

    seed corpus → run packs with successful feedback (3 rounds) →
    trellis analyze learning-candidates → operator approves a row →
    trellis curate promote-learning → verify precedent in graph

REST drives ingest + retrieval, MCP `record_feedback` carries the per-item helpful signal, the new CLI subcommands run as subprocesses against the same cloud config the spawned uvicorn uses. Only the post-promotion graph-store assertion uses a direct `StoreRegistry` — there's no CLI surface for "read a node by id."

## Notes

- `helpful_candidate["recommendation_type"]` is `promote_guidance` (not `promote_precedent`) because PackBuilder stamps `item_type="document"` for document-strategy hits; both paths are accepted by `prepare_learning_promotions`.
- Document-only loops produce candidates with empty `target_entity_ids`, so the empty-edges path is exercised. The promote command still iterates the (empty) edge list so a future `target_entity_ids`-stamping change in PackBuilder won't silently break the link half.
- The `_live_registry` env-var helper is still duplicated with #78 (H2.4). After both land, a tiny refactor PR can pull it into `tests/integration/loops/conftest.py`.

## Test plan

## Summary

Three CLI-hygiene + backend-parity fixes carried over from the H1.x hand-off list:

1. **Route CLI structlog to stderr** — `trellis_cli.main` now configures structlog via a Typer callback (`logger_factory=PrintLoggerFactory(file=sys.stderr)`). Stdout is reserved for `--format json` payloads; logs land on stderr where piping into `jq` / `json.loads` can't see them. Mirrors the equivalent `trellis.mcp.server._configure_mcp_logging` fix from #72. Wired as a callback rather than a console-script wrapper so operators don't need `pip install -e .` after upgrades.

2. **Fix Rich soft-wrap in analyze JSON output** — `trellis_cli.analyze` swaps `console.print(json.dumps(...))` for plain `print` across all 11 `--format json` paths. Rich soft-wraps long strings at terminal width, injecting literal `\r\n` inside JSON string values; the `extractor-fallbacks` subcommand was the visible victim (`xfail` in `test_subprocess_smoke`). The remaining 10 paths were time bombs waiting for a long-enough payload.

3. **SQLite content_tags filter parity** — `_build_tag_conditions` now mirrors the `IS NULL OR ... IN (...)` pattern the Postgres path got in #73. PackBuilder applies a default `signal_quality=[high, standard, low]` allowlist whenever `tag_filters` is supplied; strict-`IN` silently filtered out items that bypassed the classifier (no `signal_quality` tag). Default-pass restores cross-backend parity. Both scalar and list facets covered.

### Test side

- Removed `_strip_structlog_lines` workaround in `tests/integration/cli/conftest.py` (no longer needed — structlog is on stderr).
- `tests/unit/cli/conftest.py` switches the autouse fixture to set `TRELLIS_LOG_LEVEL=CRITICAL` via `monkeypatch.setenv` so the new callback honours the override (CliRunner merges stderr into `result.output`, so we still need active suppression for in-process unit tests).
- Removed the `xfail` on `test_analyze_extractor_fallbacks_on_empty_event_log`.
- Added two regression tests for the SQLite content_tags fix: `test_search_untagged_doc_passes_signal_quality_filter` and `test_search_untagged_doc_passes_domain_filter`.

## Summary

- **H2.2 outside-in tests** for the advisory subsystem from #65: `test_advisory_inject` (full loop end-to-end through REST) + `test_advisory_suppress` (cutoff filter verification via direct `AdvisoryStore` writes). Mirrors the inside-out `agent_loop_convergence` scenario at the public surface.
- **REST `/api/v1/packs` now surfaces advisories.** `PackBuilder` was constructed without `advisory_store` and `PackResponse` lacked an `advisories` field — the headline feature from #65 was structurally absent on REST (only MCP had it). This PR wires it the same way the MCP server does.
- **Three production fixes** the test work surfaced — all called out in the commit body.

## Production fixes

### 1. REST advisory wiring (`routes/retrieve.py` + `trellis_wire/dtos.py`)

`assemble_pack` now lazy-loads `AdvisoryStore` from `<stores_dir>/advisories.json` if present, mirroring the MCP server's pattern. `PackResponse` gains `advisories: list[dict] = []`. Existing callers see no behavior change (default empty list); new callers see attached advisories per pack scope.

### 2. PostgresDocumentStore.search left connections idle in transaction

With `autocommit=False`, the implicit transaction stayed open after every SELECT. Surfaced via `pg_stat_activity` while debugging the deadlock — three uvicorn-side connections in `idle in transaction` state with stale SELECTs in the query column. Added `self.conn.commit()` after the cursor closes. Independently correct regardless of the deadlock fix.

### 3. `spawn_uvicorn` deadlocked uvicorn on the third request

Used `stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE` without a draining thread. Trellis's structlog defaults to `PrintLoggerFactory` (writes to stdout via `print(..., flush=True)`) — after ~3 `/packs` calls the OS pipe buffer (~8KB on Windows) filled, and the next `print()` blocked forever inside `structlog/_output.py:110`.

py-spy on the uvicorn anyio worker thread pinned the exact frame:

```
msg (structlog/_output.py:110)
_proxy_to_logger (structlog/_base.py:223)
meth (structlog/_native.py:164)
append (trellis/stores/postgres/event_log.py:79)
emit (trellis/stores/base/event_log.py:198)
_emit_telemetry (trellis/retrieve/pack_builder.py:611)
build (trellis/retrieve/pack_builder.py:326)
assemble_pack (trellis_api/routes/retrieve.py:65)
```

Switched `spawn_uvicorn` to redirect stdout+stderr to a per-test `<tmp_path>/uvicorn.log`, with `_read_log` for error reporting in `wait_for_healthz`. Same fix benefits all live-API tests; would have eventually flaked the noise-demote loop too once it grew past two pack-builds.

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/integration/loops/ tests/integration/api/ -v` — 16 pass against live Neon + AuraDB (38s for the 2 H2.2 tests; 227s for the full live-API suite).
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/api/test_routes.py tests/unit/api/test_logging.py -v` — 45 pass; PackResponse field addition is backward-compatible.
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/stores/test_postgres_stores.py tests/unit/sdk/ -v` — 53 pass + 31 skipped (gated). The doc-store commit fix doesn't break any existing test.
- [ ] Reviewer: `set -a && source .env && set +a && pytest tests/integration/loops/test_advisory_loop.py -v` — both tests should pass against your live infra.

## Out of scope (follow-ups)

- A handful of pre-existing lint warnings in `tests/integration/_live_server.py` (TRY003/EM102 on RuntimeError messages, RUF100 unused noqa) — unchanged by this PR; consistent with prior style.

body:
## Summary

Closes the two remaining items in [TODO.md](TODO.md)'s "Eval framework gaps surfaced" section, both observed during the live-data eval run on 2026-04-27.

### 1. pgvector dim mismatch — fail fast at construction

The Neon test DB had `vectors.embedding` provisioned at `vector(3)` by an earlier unit-test fixture run; eval scenarios default to dim=16. `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` is a no-op against an existing table, so the store silently inherited the old dim and crashed on the first upsert with `DataException: expected 3 dimensions, not 16`.

[`PgVectorStore._init_schema`](src/trellis/stores/pgvector/store.py) now reads the existing column dim from `pg_attribute` after the CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS and raises `ValueError` if it doesn't match `self._dimensions`. The error message tells the caller exactly what they hit and offers two options: pass the matching dim, or DROP TABLE and let the store recreate it.

### 2. AuraDB vector index cohabitation — document the constraint

Neo4j allows only one vector index per `(label, property)` pair. On a shared AuraDB instance, the unit-test fixture, the eval scenarios, and the loader script all want `(:Node).embedding` — the second `CREATE VECTOR INDEX <other-name> IF NOT EXISTS` is silently a no-op and the ONLINE-wait then times out at 30s.

New "Vector index cohabitation" section in [`docs/deployment/neo4j-auradb.md`](docs/deployment/neo4j-auradb.md) explains the rule, which index name + dim each consumer uses, what the loader does about it, and the practical recommendation (separate AuraDB Free instances for eval vs unit tests). Two new troubleshooting rows cover the symptoms a stranger would hit. Cross-references the new pgvector construction-time check for the parallel rule on Postgres.

## Test plan

- [x] **Live pgvector contract suite against Neon:** 43/43 in `tests/unit/stores/test_pgvector.py` + `tests/unit/stores/contracts/test_pgvector_contract.py`.
- [x] **Two new tests in `TestDimMismatchFailsFast`** cover the new failure path: matching-dim succeeds, mismatched-dim raises with the dim in the message.
- [x] `ruff check` clean; `ruff format` clean; `mypy` clean.

## What this PR does NOT touch

- The Neo4j vector store has the same shape (existing index options aren't honored on a second CREATE), but the symptom is already covered by the new troubleshooting row + the existing `VectorIndexNotOnlineError` from `wait_for_vector_index_online`. A symmetric fail-fast on the Neo4j side would need a `SHOW VECTOR INDEXES YIELD options` follow-up; deferred until a stranger trips over it.

body:
## Summary

Mirrors [#62](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/62)'s SQLite fix against Postgres. The previous implementation looped `upsert_node` / `upsert_edge` with `self.conn.commit()` per row — every row paid for a `get_node` SELECT, one INSERT (and an UPDATE if a prior version existed), and a commit round trip. On managed Postgres (Neon) those round trips dominate; the same hot spot SQLite paid in fsyncs, Postgres pays in network RTTs.

Both rewrites follow the same shape: pre-validate, bulk-fetch existing rows once, close priors in one `UPDATE ... = ANY(%s)`, INSERT all new versions via `executemany`, single commit at end.

## Numbers

**Standalone benchmark against live Neon Postgres:**

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Fresh 1K-node bulk | ~1-5 nodes/sec | **1794 nodes/sec** (~300-1000×) |
| Overlap 1K-node bulk | ~1-5 nodes/sec | **1207 nodes/sec** |
| Fresh 4K-edge bulk | n/a | **2709 edges/sec** |

SCD-2 still works: 2 versions after re-ingest (verified inline).

## Atomicity

- All statements (UPDATE close + `executemany` INSERT) run under one implicit transaction. If any statement fails, the unflushed changes are rolled back at commit time — **strictly stronger atomicity** than the prior per-row commit loop, which could leave the batch half-written on a mid-loop error.
- In-batch duplicate `(source, target, edge_type)` triplets are rejected up-front by `_pre_validate_edges_bulk`; the new code relies on the lookup map being unique-per-key.

## Test plan

- [x] **Live Postgres contract suite against Neon:** 72/72 in [`tests/unit/stores/contracts/test_postgres_graph_contract.py`](tests/unit/stores/contracts/test_postgres_graph_contract.py) — covers SCD-2 close-then-create, role-immutability atomic check, atomic-validation no-partial-write contract, `as_of` time travel, and the canonical DSL.
- [x] `ruff check` clean; `ruff format` clean; `mypy` clean
- [x] Standalone benchmark on live Neon as above

## Why no scenario 5.3 measurement

Running scenario 5.3 with `TRELLIS_KNOWLEDGE_PG_DSN` set hits the [pre-existing pgvector dim-mismatch gap](TODO.md) (Neon column is `vector(3)`, scenarios default to `dim=16`). That's a separate eval-framework item already tracked in TODO.md — not blocking this PR's correctness signal, which comes from the contract suite.

## What this PR does NOT touch

- `executemany` on psycopg sends one SQL statement per row over the wire. A future optimisation would use psycopg 3's pipeline mode (or `COPY` for very large bulks), but the current ~1800 nodes/sec is well above the 100/sec floor scenario 5.3 gates on. Defer until a workload signal motivates further tuning.

## Refs

- [#62](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/62) — the SQLite fast path this mirrors

## Summary

After [#60](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/60) landed Neo4j's bulk fast path, scenario 5.3 surfaced **SQLite as the new slowest backend** at ~32 nodes/sec on the populated-graph workload. Profiling found two hot spots in [`src/trellis/stores/sqlite/graph.py`](src/trellis/stores/sqlite/graph.py):

- `upsert_nodes_bulk` looped `upsert_node` with `commit=True` per row → one fsync per node, one `get_node` SELECT per node.
- `upsert_edges_bulk` did the same with `upsert_edge`.

Both rewrites follow the same shape: pre-validate, bulk-fetch existing rows once, close any priors in one `UPDATE ... IN (...)`, INSERT all new versions via `executemany`, single commit at end.

## Numbers

**Standalone benchmark (in-process tmp SQLite):**

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Fresh 1K-node bulk | ~32 nodes/sec | **33,464 nodes/sec** (~1000×) |
| Overlap 1K-node bulk | ~32 nodes/sec | **13,024 nodes/sec** |
| Fresh 5K-node bulk | n/a | **29,092 nodes/sec** |

## Atomicity

- All statements (UPDATE close + `executemany` INSERT) run under one implicit transaction. If any statement fails, the unflushed changes are rolled back at commit time — **strictly stronger atomicity** than the prior per-row commit loop, which could leave the batch half-written on a mid-loop error.
- In-batch duplicate `(source, target, edge_type)` triplets are rejected up-front by `_pre_validate_edges_bulk`; the new code relies on the lookup map being unique-per-key (matches the existing contract).

body:
## Summary

Third and last of the Phase 3 unblocks from the live-data eval run on 2026-04-27. The bulk node ingest path was running `OPTIONAL MATCH` per row inside the UNWIND even when no prior version existed — paying for an indexed lookup, a `valid_to` filter, a no-op `SET old.valid_to`, and a `coalesce` on every row of every batch. This PR branches the write Cypher based on what the role-immutability pre-fetch already told us.

**Standalone live measurement against AuraDB Free, 2026-04-27:**

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Fresh 1K-node bulk | ~45 nodes/sec | **2136 nodes/sec** |
| Fresh 5K-node bulk | n/a | **3643 nodes/sec** |
| Overlap 1K bulk (slow path) | ~45 nodes/sec | **1893 nodes/sec** |
| Loader's raw UNWIND (same instance, no governance) | 3281 nodes/sec | — |

The store's hot path now matches and slightly exceeds the loader script's raw-UNWIND path on the same AuraDB instance. The cold path (overlapping `node_id`s) is also faster because moving `created_at` into `row.props` collapses the previous two `SET`s into one for both branches.

## How

- The pre-fetch round trip is **unchanged** — it has to run first to honour [`test_upsert_nodes_bulk_atomic_role_immutability_check`](tests/unit/stores/contracts/graph_store_contract.py). The contract requires a precise per-row error before any write.
- When the pre-fetch returns no existing roles, the write Cypher branches to a CREATE-only UNWIND. Same shape as the loader script.
- When the pre-fetch finds at least one prior current row, the write keeps the full OPTIONAL MATCH shape so SCD-2 close-then-create still applies. `coalesce(old.created_at, ...)` carries the original `created_at` forward.
- `created_at` is now set in Python as part of `row.props` so the hot path lands every property in one `SET n = row.props`. The cold path keeps the follow-up `SET n.created_at = created_at_carry` to override with the coalesce result.

## Race-window note

Between the pre-fetch and the CREATE-only write, a concurrent writer could create a current row for one of these `node_id`s — the hot path would then produce a duplicate current version. This matches the already-documented Community-edition concurrent-write race ([module docstring](src/trellis/stores/neo4j/graph.py)). No regression for the documented single-writer assumption.

## Tests

- New [`tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_upsert_bulk_fast_path.py`](tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_upsert_bulk_fast_path.py) — 3 unit tests via mock driver:
  - Fresh batch emits CREATE-only Cypher (no `OPTIONAL MATCH`).
  - Overlapping batch keeps `OPTIONAL MATCH` and `coalesce`.
  - Role-immutability conflict in the overlap aborts before any write (`session.execute_write.assert_not_called()`).

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_upsert_bulk_fast_path.py -v` → 3 passed
- [x] All Neo4j unit tests pass: `pytest tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_*.py -q` → 30 passed
- [x] `ruff check` clean; `ruff format` clean; `mypy` clean
- [x] **Closed loop on AuraDB Free:** standalone 1K and 5K bulk measurements as shown above; SCD-2 history verified (2 versions after re-ingest of the same `node_id`).

## Follow-up note on scenario 5.3

The scenario's `ingest_nodes_per_sec.neo4j` metric still shows ~40 nodes/sec post-fix because the scenario's `_ingest()` calls `vector_store.upsert()` in a 200-row Python loop (200 round trips × ~70ms = ~14s), which dominates wall time. Switching it to `vector_store.upsert_bulk` is a separate fix — out of scope here.

- [`plan-neo4j-hardening.md` §5.1(b)](docs/design/plan-neo4j-hardening.md) — the Phase 3 deferred item this resolves

body:
## Summary

The first live-data eval run flagged that [`Neo4jVectorStore.query()`](src/trellis/stores/neo4j/vector.py) calls `db.index.vector.queryNodes`, which AuraDB has marked deprecated:

> warn: feature deprecated with replacement. db.index.vector.queryNodes is deprecated. It is replaced by SEARCH.

Every vector query emitted a `DEPRECATION` notification — dozens per scenario 5.3 run. This PR migrates to the [Cypher 25 SEARCH clause](https://neo4j.com/docs/cypher-manual/current/clauses/search/), which is the Neo4j-recommended replacement and will outlive the procedure.

## What changed

- `Neo4jVectorStore.query()` now emits:
  ```cypher
  MATCH (n:Node)
  SEARCH n IN ( VECTOR INDEX <name> FOR $vector LIMIT $k ) SCORE AS score
  WHERE n.valid_to IS NULL
  RETURN n.node_id AS item_id, score, n.vector_metadata_json AS metadata_json
  ```
- Index name is **inlined as a Cypher literal** — the `VECTOR INDEX` reference doesn't accept bound parameters. `self._index` is already validated at construction time (same constraint the CREATE DDL in `_init_schema` follows).
- `valid_to IS NULL` lives in the **post-SEARCH WHERE**, not the index-WHERE. The vector index only honours properties registered as `additionalFilterProperties` at CREATE time. Empirically verified against AuraDB 2026-04-27 — pushing the filter down errored with `PropertyNotFound: ... not an additional property for vector search with filters`. Pushdown is a separate optimisation gated on its own evidence.
- Module docstring bumps the minimum Neo4j version to **2025.06** (Cypher 25 introduction). AuraDB always runs the latest; self-hosted Docker users on `neo4j:5` should upgrade to `neo4j:2025` or newer.

## Tests

- New [`tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector_query_cypher.py`](tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector_query_cypher.py) — 4 unit tests asserting on the generated Cypher via a `MagicMock` driver:
  - The deprecated procedure is gone, the SEARCH clause is present.
  - Index name flows through as a Cypher literal (custom-name case).
  - Post-WHERE comes after `SCORE AS`.
  - Over-fetch factor is honoured when filters are present.

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector_query_cypher.py -v` → 4 passed
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_driver_lifecycle.py -v` → 15 passed (no regression)
- [x] `ruff check` clean; `ruff format` clean; `mypy` clean
- [x] **Closed loop on AuraDB Free**: seed 3 nodes + embeddings via the public API, run `vector.query()`:
  - Top-k returns expected cosine ordering: a (1.0) > b (0.997) > c (0.5)
  - Historical SCD-2 versions excluded (updated `a` no longer appears)
  - **Zero `DEPRECATION` notifications** during the run (was: one per query call)

## What this does NOT fix

- The third deferred Phase 3 item — `upsert_nodes_bulk` raw-UNWIND fast path — is independent and still warn-level on scenario 5.3 (~45 nodes/sec). Out of scope here.

## Refs

- [Neo4j Cypher 25 SEARCH clause](https://neo4j.com/docs/cypher-manual/current/clauses/search/)
- [Vector indexes — Neo4j docs](https://neo4j.com/docs/cypher-manual/current/indexes/semantic-indexes/vector-indexes/)
- Plan reference: [`plan-neo4j-hardening.md` §5.1(c)](docs/design/plan-neo4j-hardening.md) (added in PR #57)

body:
## Summary

The first live-data eval run (2026-04-27) measured **recall@10 = 0.36** for vector queries against AuraDB Free — well below the 0.95 floor scenario 5.3 gates on. AuraDB defaults vector indexes to `quantization=true`, which collapses recall on low-dimensional vectors (the eval workload is dim=16, 200 embeddings). After rebuilding the index with `quantization=false` on the same data: **recall@10 = 1.00**.

This PR exposes `m`, `ef_construction`, and `quantization` as constructor kwargs on `Neo4jVectorStore`, picks `quantization=False` as the Trellis default, and bakes the three values into the `CREATE VECTOR INDEX` `OPTIONS` map.

## What changed

- New constructor kwargs on [`Neo4jVectorStore`](src/trellis/stores/neo4j/vector.py): `m: int = 16`, `ef_construction: int = 100`, `quantization: bool = False`.
- `_init_schema` now emits all three in the `OPTIONS { indexConfig: { ... } }` map. The Cypher DDL doesn't accept bound parameters for options, so they go in as literals — all inputs are validated in `__init__` so inlining is safe.
- Validation: zero / negative `m` or `ef_construction` raises `ValueError` before any network call.
- No `StoreRegistry` change needed — `cls(**params)` passes any new kwargs straight through. Config users can write:
  ```yaml
  knowledge:
    vector:
      backend: neo4j
      ...
      m: 32
      ef_construction: 200
      quantization: false
  ```
- **Existing indexes unchanged.** `CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS` is still a no-op against a live index; swapping knobs requires a manual `DROP INDEX` first. Documented inline.

## Tests

- New [`tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector_index_options.py`](tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector_index_options.py) — 8 unit tests asserting on the generated Cypher via a `MagicMock` driver. Live tests aren't viable here because AuraDB enforces only one vector index per `(:label, property)` — a uniquely-named test index collides with the persistent unit-suite index and `CREATE` silently no-ops.
- Two negative tests added in [`tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector.py`](tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector.py) (env-gated alongside the rest of the live suite) for the constructor validation guard.

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector_index_options.py -v` → 8 passed
- [x] `pytest tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_driver_lifecycle.py -v` → 15 passed (no regression)
- [x] `ruff check` clean on touched files; `ruff format` clean; `mypy` clean
- [x] **Closed loop on AuraDB Free**: dropped existing indexes, re-ran scenario 5.3 → `vector_recall_at_10.neo4j: 1.0` (was 0.36 with quantization=true). The HNSW recall warning gate disappeared from the report.

## What this does NOT fix

- Ingest throughput is still ~45 nodes/sec (warn-level on the 100/sec floor) — that's the next deferred item, `upsert_nodes_bulk` raw-UNWIND fast path. Out of scope here.
- The `db.index.vector.queryNodes is deprecated` warning still fires on every vector query — also a separate PR.

Re-lands the deferred Phase 1.3 deliverable from
[plan-neo4j-hardening.md](docs/design/plan-neo4j-hardening.md).
Original PR was [#23](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/23)
(closed without merging when the hardening series was deferred).
Now unblocked by [#51](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/51)
(Phase 1.1 — driver lifecycle + cached `_neo4j_drivers`).

## Summary

Today, if Neo4j is unreachable, uvicorn starts cleanly and the first
request fails with a Bolt error. With this change, an operator can
opt into a startup connectivity check that turns "unreachable Neo4j"
into a fail-fast `RegistryValidationError` aggregated alongside
config errors.

### `stores/neo4j/base.py`
- New `verify_connectivity(driver)` wrapper around the Neo4j driver's
  `Driver.verify_connectivity()` method. Wrapper exists so callers
  have a stable import surface.

### `stores/registry.py`
- New `TRELLIS_VALIDATE_CONNECTIVITY` env var + `_resolve_connectivity_check`
  helper (truthy `1` / `true` / `yes` / `on`).
- `StoreRegistry.validate` gains `check_connectivity: bool | None = None`
  kwarg. None (default) respects the env var; True / False overrides.
- When enabled, `_check_neo4j_connectivity` pings every cached Neo4j
  driver (Phase 1.1's `_neo4j_drivers` cache, so shared drivers ping
  once not twice). Failures become tuples in the same
  `RegistryValidationError` aggregate as config errors, with label
  `"neo4j-driver:<uri>"`.
- Connectivity branch is a no-op when there are no cached Neo4j drivers
  (non-Neo4j deployments pay zero cost).

### `app.py`: not touched
The lifespan already calls `validate()`; the env-var path means
production opts in via env config rather than code change. Cleanest
separation between "code" and "deployment posture."

## Tests
- `tests/unit/stores/test_registry_connectivity.py` — 23 pure-unit
  tests across 5 classes: env-var resolver matrix, default behavior
  (no ping), explicit True pings each cached driver, shared driver
  pinged once (regression on Phase 1.1 sharing), connectivity
  failures aggregate alongside config failures, env-var enables ping
  without kwarg.
- `tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_connectivity_live.py` — 4 env-gated
  live tests against AuraDB.

  driver lifecycle, connectivity, close, vector index wait).
- [x] Full unit suite: 2267 passed, 0 failed (was 2244 after #51;
  delta = +23).
- [x] `ruff check` + `mypy` clean.
