body:
## Summary

Re-land of the Phase 1.1 design originally drafted in PR #21 (commit `216d74b`), rewritten against post-#39 (`Neo4jSessionRunner`) and post-#41 (consolidated bulk paths) main rather than cherry-picked. The original commit conflicted across 4 files because it predated those landings.

Closes Phase 1.1 of `docs/design/plan-neo4j-hardening.md`. The bare driver construction was the leakiest item in the Neo4j hardening list — zero production-relevant kwargs were flowing through, and two stores against one instance were creating two connection pools.

### What ships

- **`DriverConfig`** (frozen dataclass) in `stores/neo4j/base.py` with production-safe defaults: `connection_timeout=30s`, `max_connection_pool_size=100`, `max_transaction_retry_time=30s`, `keep_alive=True`, `user_agent="trellis-ai"`. Each field documented with the why-this-default rationale.
- **`build_driver(uri, user, password, *, config=None)`** applies the config. Backward-compatible: working AuraDB connections see the same behavior; a misconfigured network now surfaces a 30s connect failure instead of hanging indefinitely.
- **Driver injection contract** on `Neo4jGraphStore` + `Neo4jVectorStore` constructors — accept either `password + driver_config` (build + own) or `driver` (caller-injected; `close()` is a no-op). Mixing raises `ValueError`. Tracked via `_owns_driver`.
- **`StoreRegistry`** caches one driver per `(uri, user)` so the graph + vector pair against the same instance shares one connection pool. `_inject_neo4j_driver` extracts `password` + `driver_config`, builds (or reuses) the driver, injects `driver=` into store kwargs. `driver_config` accepts a `DriverConfig`, a dict, or omitted (defaults). First-call wins for a given `(uri, user)`.
- **`StoreRegistry.close()`** now closes shared Neo4j drivers after closing individual stores. Errors in any single `close()` are logged + skipped so a misbehaving backend can't block cleanup of the rest.

### What's NOT in scope

Phase 1.3 (`validate()` pings Neo4j on startup) is the natural follow-up but stays scoped to its own PR. Pre-existing ruff format drift in `graph.py` (unrelated to driver lifecycle, lives in code that pre-dated PR #39) is left alone — fixing it would muddy this diff.

## Test plan

- [x] `uv run pytest tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_base.py tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_driver_lifecycle.py -v` — 25/25 new pure-unit tests pass
- [x] `uv run pytest tests/unit/ -q` — 2244 passed, 276 skipped, 0 failed (delta: +25 from new tests)
- [x] `uv run ruff check src/trellis/stores/ tests/unit/stores/` — clean
- [x] `uv run ruff format --check` on the files this PR touches — clean
- [x] `uv run mypy src/trellis/stores/` — clean
- [ ] Live AuraDB suite — recommend running `pytest tests/integration -m neo4j_live` against AuraDB Free in CI/local before merge to confirm no regressions to the existing graph/vector/contract/e2e behavior. The original commit (216d74b) reported 116/116 live tests passing; this re-land should match.

## New tests

`tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_base.py` (10 tests):
- `DriverConfig` defaults match documented production-safe values
- Frozen contract (cannot mutate after construction)
- Override surface for individual fields
- Two default instances are equal (hashable for cache-key use)
- `build_driver` passes URI + auth correctly
- Default config kwargs flow through to `GraphDatabase.driver`
- Custom config overrides flow through
- Returns whatever `GraphDatabase.driver` returns
- `check_driver_installed` no-ops when `neo4j` is installed
- `check_driver_installed` raises with install hint when `HAS_NEO4J=False`

`tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_driver_lifecycle.py` (15 tests):
- Graph + vector store own the driver they build (closed by `store.close()`)
- `driver_config` flows through to `build_driver` from store constructors
- Stores skip closing on injected drivers (`_owns_driver=False`)
- Constructors reject `driver + password` and `driver + driver_config`
- Constructors require `password` when no `driver` injected
- Registry injects one shared driver across graph + vector for the same URI
- `registry.close()` closes the shared driver exactly once
- `registry.close()` survives a single store's `close()` raising
- Dict-shaped `driver_config` in registry params becomes a `DriverConfig`
- Invalid `driver_config` types raise a clear `TypeError`

Re-lands the deferred Phase 2.3 deliverable from
[plan-neo4j-hardening.md](docs/design/plan-neo4j-hardening.md).
Original PR was #27 (closed when the hardening series was deferred).

## Summary

Operators can now move graph data between any two backends Trellis
supports without losing it.

### `src/trellis/migrate/` (new package)
- **`GraphMigrator(source, dest)`** — walks the source's current
  graph state via the public `GraphStore` API only (`query`,
  `get_aliases`, `get_edges`) and replays it through the destination's
  `upsert_node` / `upsert_alias` / `upsert_edge`. Backend-agnostic:
  SQLite ↔ Postgres ↔ Neo4j all work without backend-specific
  branches.
- **`MigrationReport`** — counts (read/written/skipped) for nodes,
  edges, aliases + `elapsed_ms` + `dry_run` flag + error list. One-line
  `summary()` for CLI output.
- **`MigrationCapacityExceededError`** — raised when source has more
  current nodes than `max_nodes` (default 100K). POC scope is an
  in-memory snapshot; paginated iteration lands when a deployment
  asks.
- **Idempotent on retry**: destination row matching (node_id, or alias
  source/raw_id, or current edge source/target/type) is skipped, not
  overwritten.
- Walks outgoing edges only and dedupes by `edge_id` so each edge is
  visited exactly once.

### `src/trellis_cli/admin.py`
- New `trellis admin migrate-graph` command.
- `--from-config <yaml>` + `--to-config <yaml>` — each YAML file
  contains a single `graph:` block matching the recommended-config
  shape, so source and destination are independent.
- `--dry-run`, `--max-nodes`, `--format text|json`.
- Closes destination first then source so source diagnostics survive
  a flaky destination close.
- Capacity errors and per-row failures both produce non-zero exit
  codes for CI use.

### `docs/design/plan-neo4j-hardening.md`
- 2.3 marked landed.

## Tests
- 12 unit round-trip tests (SQLite ↔ SQLite): basic copy, idempotency,
  dry-run, edge cases (empty/lonely/self-referential/dedup), capacity
  overflow, summary formatting.
- 6 CLI smoke tests: text/JSON/dry-run output, missing-config and
  invalid-shape error paths, capacity exit code.
- 3 env-gated SQLite → Neo4j live tests proving end-to-end migration
  against AuraDB.

Cherry-picked from the deferred Neo4j hardening series PR [#24](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/24), scoped down to drop the \`DriverConfig\` dependency from the also-deferred PR [#21](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/21).

## Why

AuraDB (and self-hosted Neo4j on slow disks) provisions vector indexes asynchronously: \`CREATE VECTOR INDEX\` returns immediately, but the index isn't queryable until its background population completes. The first \`db.index.vector.queryNodes\` call against an unfinished index fails with \"no such vector schema index\" — the same race the A.1 e2e suite already worked around by reusing a persistent index.

This PR closes the race in production code so the workaround is no longer needed.

## What landed

- **\`wait_for_vector_index_online(driver, *, database, index_name, timeout=30, poll_interval=0.5)\`** in \`neo4j/base.py\`. Polls \`SHOW VECTOR INDEXES\` until ONLINE; fast-fails on FAILED; tolerates the not-yet-visible window AuraDB has in the first ~100ms after CREATE.
- **\`VectorIndexNotOnlineError\`** carries the last observed state + populationPercent so an operator can distinguish \"still populating\" from \"stuck FAILED\" from \"never appeared\".
- **\`Neo4jVectorStore.__init__\`** accepts \`index_wait_timeout=30.0\`; \`_init_schema\` calls the wait helper after CREATE.

## Test plan

- [x] \`uv run ruff check src/trellis/stores/neo4j/\` — clean
- [x] \`uv run mypy src/trellis/stores/neo4j/\` — clean
- [x] 7 pure-unit tests covering the polling state machine
- [x] **104/104 live Neo4j tests pass against AuraDB Free** (33 graph contract + 25 vector + 39 graph + 7 new index-wait)

## Why this is a cherry-pick, not a merge of #24

The full hardening series (#21-#27) was written before main shipped \`Neo4jSessionRunner\` (#39), bulk methods (#34/#35/#37/#41), and the simplified bulk helpers (#44). #24 originally added \`driver\` / \`driver_config\` constructor params to \`Neo4jVectorStore\` (from PR #21) which we're not landing. This PR keeps just the index-wait surface — orthogonal to driver lifecycle and a real production correctness fix.

The remaining hardening items are tracked as deferred in TODO.md (added in the companion plan-doc PR).

POC stage; no backwards compat needed. Straight cut-over.

body:
Single consolidated PR replacing the closed #42 and #43, simplified after auditing the original design.

## Two cleanups

### 1. Drop `alias_upserted` per-row debug logs

[#38](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/38) removed the equivalent `node_upserted` / `edge_upserted` / `vector_upserted` lines but missed `alias_upserted` in the three graph backends (Neo4j, SQLite, Postgres). Same justification:

- No test asserts on these log lines.
- `MutationExecutor` emits structured EventLog events for actual auditability.
- A bulk path looping over `upsert_alias` would emit one debug line per row, drowning real signal.

### 2. Factor the duplicated bulk required-key check

8 callsites had pasted the same loop:

```python
for i, spec in enumerate(items):
    for key in (...):
        if key not in spec or spec[key] is None:
            raise ValueError(f"<method>[{i}]: missing required key {key!r}")
```

Replaced with `_validate_bulk_required_keys(items, keys, method_name)` as a `@staticmethod` on each of `VectorStore` and `GraphStore`. Lives next to the existing `_pre_validate_bulk_item_ids` / `_pre_validate_edges_bulk` siblings on the same ABCs.

## Why this design and not the [#43](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/43) one

The closed #43 created a new `trellis.stores.base._bulk_validation` module with one shared free function. Two problems on review:

1. **Underscore-prefixed module broke codebase convention** — no other module in `src/trellis/` uses one. The "private" signal is wrong since the helper is imported across 6 packages.
2. **The indirection cost (new file + 8 cross-package imports) was larger than the duplication it removed.**

Two 5-line `@staticmethod` bodies on the two ABCs is the right amount of factoring for a foundation-stage codebase. POC stage; the helpers are next to the validators they're used alongside, which is the natural place to find them.

## One deliberate behavior tightening

`_pre_validate_nodes_bulk` previously checked only `"node_type" not in spec`, allowing `{"node_type": None}` to slip past the validator and fail downstream with a NOT NULL violation. The new helper rejects `None` too. Strict improvement — no caller could have relied on the looser behavior, and POC stage means no backwards-compat constraint.

## Replaced sites

body:
## Summary

- `Neo4jGraphStore.upsert_nodes_bulk` and `upsert_edges_bulk` now run all of their round trips under a single shared session, dropping per-round-trip session-acquisition cost.
- `upsert_edges_bulk` folds `coalesce(old.edge_id, ...)` and `coalesce(old.created_at, ...)` into the UNWIND write — the `_fetch_current_edges_bulk` round trip is deleted, taking the per-batch round-trip count from 3 down to 2.
- `upsert_nodes_bulk` keeps its pre-fetch round trip (still required for the atomic role-immutability contract — see below) but slims it to fetch only `node_role` rather than full node payloads, since `created_at` now carries forward via inline `coalesce`.
- Dead code removed: `_find_current_edge` (unused since [#35](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/35) inlined its query into the collapsed `upsert_edge` Cypher).

## Why the node bulk pre-fetch stays

The node bulk path's atomicity contract (`test_upsert_nodes_bulk_atomic_role_immutability_check`) requires that *any* role-immutability conflict in a batch reject the whole batch with no partial writes. Cypher has no clean way to abort mid-UNWIND on a per-row validation failure — `WHERE` after `WITH` only filters the failing row through, leaving the other rows to commit. Working around that needs subquery / `CALL { ... }` gymnastics that aren't worth the complexity for the modest win. So the node path keeps its validate-then-write split (now ~50% smaller payload, single session). The edge bulk path has no equivalent atomicity contract on the existing-edge state, so its pre-fetch was safe to drop entirely.

## Summary

Closes Logic Gap 1.2 from `TODO.md` (Core Loop Audit). `ClassifierPipeline.mode` was stateless and derived from `llm_classifier is not None`, with **no per-item record of which mode produced the tags**. Two pipelines run against the same content in different modes produced indistinguishable tag sets — reclassification comparisons and cross-mode-drift audit were impossible.

**Fix:** every `classify()` call stamps its mode on `MergedClassification`, and `to_content_tags()` propagates it to `ContentTags.classified_mode`. The mode reflects the pipeline's *configuration* (LLM wired or not), not which path *actually fired* on a given call — so a high-confidence deterministic result in an enrichment-mode pipeline still records `mode='enrichment'`. That matches what callers actually need to reason about: "this run had LLM available" vs "this run was deterministic only."

A new `ClassifierMode = Literal["ingestion", "enrichment"]` alias in `schemas/classification.py` keeps the vocabulary in one place. `ContentTags.classified_mode` is `None` on legacy / hand-edited records — same handling as `classified_at` (Gap 1.1).

**Deliberately not added:** a `CLASSIFIER_MODE_CHANGED` event. `ClassifierPipeline` doesn't expose a runtime setter for `_llm_classifier`, and the existing `TAGS_REFRESHED` event already carries before/after `ContentTags` — the refresh path will show mode swaps automatically. A new event for the runtime-mutation case would be speculative machinery without a caller.

## Files

- `src/trellis/schemas/classification.py` — add `ClassifierMode` type alias + `classified_mode` field on `ContentTags`.
- `src/trellis/classify/protocol.py` — add `mode` field on `MergedClassification`; propagate to `ContentTags` in `to_content_tags()`.
- `src/trellis/classify/pipeline.py` — `mode` property typed as `ClassifierMode`; `classify()` stamps it on the result.
- `tests/unit/classify/test_pipeline.py` — 6 new tests in `TestPipelineModeStamping`.
- `tests/unit/schemas/test_classification.py` — 4 new tests in `TestContentTagsClassifiedMode`.

Net +137/-9. No behavior change for existing callers (the new field defaults to `None` everywhere).

## Summary

Mirrors the GraphStore `upsert_*_bulk` work from [#34](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/34) for the vector plane. Eval scenarios that loop `vector_store.upsert(...)` per embedding paid ~50ms/call on AuraDB Free — at 1K embeddings (current loader default) that's ~50 seconds of round trips. This collapses N embeddings into a single Cypher UNWIND.

## Public surface

New abstract method on `VectorStore`:
```python
upsert_bulk(items: list[dict]) -> None
```

List-of-dicts shape mirrors `upsert` kwargs (`item_id`, `vector`, `metadata`). Validation runs on every row before any I/O — invalid rows raise `ValueError` with the offending list index.

## Backend implementations

- **Neo4j** — 1 round trip per batch via UNWIND. Detects rows whose node has no current version by comparing returned vs input count; raises with the offending list index pointing at the missing `item_id`.
- **LanceDB** — single `merge_insert(...).execute(data)` call across the whole batch. LanceDB natively accepts list payloads, so this is a **real bulk path**, not a loop. Pre-validates dimension consistency across the batch.
- **SQLite + pgvector** — pass-through loops over the existing per-row method. Pre-validates required keys so missing-key errors surface as `ValueError`-with-index instead of `KeyError` mid-loop.

## Contract tests

6 new tests in `VectorStoreContractTests` cover bulk creation, empty-list no-op, metadata round-trip, replace-existing-row semantics, missing-key rejection, post-bulk query visibility. Live-tested on SQLite, LanceDB, pgvector — all pass.

**Neo4j vector deviates from the contract suite** (per CLAUDE.md / `adr-canonical-graph-layer.md`: vectors live as properties on graph nodes, not an independent store). Its bulk tests are dedicated in `tests/unit/stores/test_neo4j_vector.py::TestUpsertBulk` — same surface plus the Neo4j-specific "node has no current version" error message with the offending list index.

body:
## Summary

Both single-row upsert paths in `Neo4jGraphStore` previously made two Neo4j round trips:
- `upsert_node` → `get_node()` to fetch existing version, then the upsert.
- `upsert_edge` → `_find_current_edge()` to fetch existing version, then the upsert.

The pre-fetch existed so we could (a) check role immutability for nodes, and (b) carry `edge_id` + `created_at` forward across SCD-2 versions. Both fold into a single Cypher query.

## Changes

**`upsert_node`** — `OPTIONAL MATCH` for the prior version, a `WHERE` clause to filter out role-immutability conflicts so the `CREATE` only runs when the write is legal, and `coalesce(old.created_at, $now)` to carry `created_at` forward. If the `WHERE` filters everything (rare role conflict), the query returns no rows and we fall back to the existing `get_node` + `check_node_role_immutable` pattern to build the canonical `ValueError`. Common case: **1 round trip**; conflict case: 2 round trips.

**`upsert_edge`** — same shape: endpoint `MATCH` + existing-edge `OPTIONAL MATCH` + close-old + create-new in a single query, with `coalesce(old.edge_id, $candidate)` and `coalesce(old.created_at, $now)` carrying state forward. Returns no rows when an endpoint is missing (same error message as before).

## Measured impact (AuraDB Free, US-east → local round trip)

| | Before | After | Improvement |
|---|---|---|---|
| Fresh inserts | ~5/sec | **11.6/sec** | ~2.3× |
| Updates | ~5/sec | **11.3/sec** | ~2.3× |
| Per-call latency | ~200ms (2 RT) | **~87ms** (1 RT) | halved |

Halves write latency on every governed mutation that goes through `MutationExecutor` — the `upsert_node`/`upsert_edge` paths are the per-row hot path the registry uses for single-mutation API calls.

## Test plan
- [x] **301/301** pass on the full Neo4j-touching test surface (`test_neo4j_graph.py` + `test_neo4j_vector.py` + `tests/unit/stores/contracts/`).
- [x] Same observable semantics — same error messages, same SCD-2 versioning behaviour, same `node_role` / `created_at` carry-forward. The contract test suite is the authoritative check.
- [x] `ruff check` and `mypy` clean on the changed file.

## Out of scope
Postgres + SQLite paths unchanged: their round trips are local and the 2-trip cost is negligible. The same collapse-to-1-RT pattern would need backend-specific SQL (CTE with `INSERT ... RETURNING`) and the latency win is small enough that it's not worth the code-change risk on this PR.

## Stack
Independent of [#33](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/33) (registry plane-split) and [#34](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/34) (`upsert_*_bulk`). All three target main and can land in any order.

## Summary

- **SQLite + Postgres** — pass-through loops over the existing per-row method. These backends aren't bottlenecked on network. The bulk method pre-validates required keys (so a missing key surfaces as `ValueError` with index instead of `KeyError` mid-loop) and pre-validates endpoint existence for edges (tightens the bulk contract to be consistent across backends).

13 new tests in `GraphStoreContractTests` covering: bulk creation, empty-list no-op, ID auto-generation, SCD-2 update behaviour (closes old version + creates new), role-immutability validation, invalid-role rejection, missing-endpoint rejection, missing-key rejection.

## Summary

`StoreRegistry(config=dict)` silently fell back to SQLite when given a plane-split config dict. The direct constructor stored `config` as-is, then `_resolve_backend` did `self._config.get(store_type)` at lookup time — which misses for `{"knowledge": {"graph": {...}}}` and falls through to `_default_backend` (SQLite for everything except blob). No warning was emitted.

Discovered while loading the eval dataset into AuraDB: a config that explicitly requested `backend: neo4j` for graph + vector silently produced a SQLite-only registry. Affected callers in the unmerged eval stack: `multi_backend_equivalence`, `populated_graph_performance`, `synthetic_traces` scenarios. They'll be patched in their respective branches once this fix lands.

## Fix

Have `__init__` detect plane-split / legacy-flat-wrapped / already-flat shapes and route the first two through the same `_extract_store_config` helper that `from_config_dir` already uses. The internal representation (flat `{store_type: cfg}`) is preserved — the rest of the registry is untouched.

Behaviour change is a **strict superset** of prior accepted inputs:
- **Plane-split dict** — was silently SQLite, now routes correctly.
- **Already-flat dict** — still routes correctly (regression test added).
- **Legacy `{stores: {...}}` wrapper** — still routes correctly with the existing one-shot deprecation warning.
- **Empty / None config** — still uses SQLite defaults.

body:
Doc-only PR. Chained off [#27](https://github.com/ronsse/trellis-ai/pull/27) (the last Neo4j hardening PR).

## Why this exists

Phase 1 + 2 of the Neo4j hardening plan (PRs #20-#27) are complete. **Every Phase 3 item is gated on "a workload signal we don't have yet."** The next step isn't "build more code" — it's "build evaluation infrastructure that *generates* that signal so we can decide which deferred items deserve to land."

This PR opens [`docs/design/plan-evaluation-strategy.md`](docs/design/plan-evaluation-strategy.md), which is **self-contained** so it can be picked up in a fresh session without any prior conversation context.

## Strategic question answered: where does eval live?

Three options compared:

| | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|
| **A.** Separate repo (`trellis-eval`) | Clean separation, can pin Trellis versions, can be private | Dual-repo overhead, slower iteration, discoverability |
| **B.** In Trellis, in PyPI package | Single PR for code + eval, easy discovery | Bloats user installs, couples eval to internals |
| **C.** In Trellis, **excluded** from PyPI package | Single-repo iteration speed, contributor-visible, clean exclusion via `pyproject.toml` | Eval datasets in repo are heavier than code (mitigated: small fixtures committed, large datasets generated synthetically) |

**Recommendation: C** — POC stage is the wrong time for dual-repo overhead. Extract to separate repo if (a) a design partner wants to run eval against their own fork, (b) eval surface dominates repo size / CI, or (c) eval methodology should be public while Trellis source goes private. None is true today.

## Four scenarios planned

Ordered by signal value (each maps to specific Phase 3 items it would unblock):

1. **Multi-backend equivalence** — same input across SQLite / Postgres / Neo4j, diff results. Validates the "blessed Neo4j with real Postgres alternative" claim.
2. **Synthetic traces end-to-end** — retrieval quality regression tracking against ground-truth labels.
3. **Populated-graph performance** — generates the most Phase 3 unblock signals (HNSW tuning, UNWIND bulk, EXPLAIN baselines, graph compaction thresholds).
4. **Agent loop convergence** — advisory fitness validation, workflow tier escalation justification, enrichment event-loop trigger pattern.

## Reuses existing surface

`src/trellis/retrieve/evaluate.py` already provides `evaluate_pack()`, scenarios, profiles, and a five-dimension scoring framework. The CLI `trellis analyze pack-quality` already exists. The new plan **builds on** these rather than starting from zero — what's missing is the persistent scenario corpus, multi-backend harness, performance baselines, and regression tracking.

## What this plan deliberately rejects

- Separate `trellis-eval` repository (premature)
- Shipping eval in the PyPI package (bloats installs, leaks internals)
- External frameworks (BEIR, MTEB, RAGAS) — surface mismatch with Trellis's pack-builder + advisory + tuner shape
- Auto-running eval on every PR (heavyweight; schedule + manual dispatch are correct triggers)

## Hand-off protocol

Self-contained section §9 in the plan tells a fresh agent exactly what to read, in what order, before writing any code. Includes the smoke commands to confirm baseline state.

## Test plan

Doc-only. Cross-references hand-checked against existing files.

## Summary

Adds [`docs/design/plan-neo4j-hardening.md`](docs/design/plan-neo4j-hardening.md) — the plan doc for making Neo4j a credible blessed graph backend for both local and cloud, with Postgres as a real alternative. Linked from `implementation-roadmap.md` so it's the entry point for any agent picking up Neo4j work.

The plan codifies the project's positioning shift (Neo4j as blessed default for local + cloud) and translates it into a tiered plan that respects POC-stage scope discipline:

- **Phase 1** — credibility (driver lifecycle, close-on-shutdown, validate-pings, vector-index wait-for-online, Community race documentation). The bare minimum so a stranger following the recommended path hits no avoidable surprises.
- **Phase 2** — Postgres as a real alternative (combined-plane config example, local + cloud onboarding docs, `trellis admin migrate-graph` tool). The "still offer Postgres" claim becomes true the day Phase 2.3 ships.
- **Phase 3** — deferred until a workload signal arrives. Every item named with the gate that would unblock it.

Also names what we deliberately reject (auto-default to Neo4j on init, perf work without a workload, multi-tenant Neo4j, async driver) with a one-line rationale each.

Recommended execution order is in §7. Total ~3 days of focused work, ~5 PRs, each phase item independently shippable.

## Summary

- Closes the remaining triage gaps from the Core Loop Audit (**2.2**, **3.1**, **4.2**, **4.4**) plus staged telemetry analyzers (**3.4**, **4.3**) and assembly-time pack quality evaluation. Signal-first posture: every new knob defaults to observation only; demote / sweep / compact actions are explicit opt-ins.
- POC discipline throughout — shadow-eval mode, archive-to-blob, count-based retention, and auto-scheduled loops deliberately deferred.
- 1888 unit tests pass; ruff + mypy clean on full src.

## Type of change

- [x] New feature
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Refactor (no behavior change)
- [ ] Docs / examples / skills only
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling

## Affected surface

- [x] CLI (`trellis`) — new ``analyze pack-telemetry`` + ``analyze extractor-fallbacks``
- [x] Stores (trace / graph / vector / blob / event log) — ``GraphStore.compact_versions``, ``BlobStore.sweep_expired``, ``BlobStore.put(..., expires_at=...)``, new event types
- [x] Classification / retrieval / pack builder — pluggable ``TokenCounter``, pack quality evaluator hook
- [x] Schemas — ``CompactionReport``, ``BlobGCReport``, ``QualityReport`` (``TrellisModel``, ``extra=\"forbid\"``)

## What landed

**3.1 Token-budget boundary accuracy.** Pluggable ``TokenCounter`` Protocol (new ``retrieve/token_counting.py``), default ``HeuristicTokenCounter`` preserves prior 4-cpt behavior. ``PackBuilder`` gains ``token_counter``, ``token_budget_safety_margin``, ``token_budget_validator``; validator emits real vs. estimated totals + delta in ``PACK_ASSEMBLED`` so estimator drift is observable without swapping counters.

**3.4 PACK_ASSEMBLED telemetry consumption.** New ``retrieve/telemetry.py::analyze_pack_telemetry()`` reports budget-saturation, rejection reasons, per-strategy yield + findings. CLI: ``trellis analyze pack-telemetry``. Read-only.

**4.3 Extractor tier fallback opacity.** Dispatcher emits ``EXTRACTOR_FALLBACK`` on ``prefer_tier_override`` and ``empty_result``. New ``extract/telemetry.py::analyze_extractor_fallbacks()`` flags sources with ≥50% fallback over ≥10 dispatches. CLI: ``trellis analyze extractor-fallbacks``.

**4.2 SCD2 retention/compaction.** ``GraphStore.compact_versions(before, *, dry_run=False, event_log=None) -> CompactionReport`` drops closed rows across ``nodes`` / ``edges`` / ``entity_aliases`` in a single transaction; current rows never touched. Implementations on SQLite + Postgres. New ``GRAPH_VERSIONS_COMPACTED`` event.

**2.2 Auto-rollback for promoted parameters.** New ``learning/tuners/rollback.py`` with ``PostPromotionPolicy``, ``PostPromotionReport``, ``monitor_post_promotion()``, ``run_post_promotion_sweep()``. Resolves baseline via ``PARAMS_UPDATED`` event, compares success rates in equivalent windows, emits new ``PARAMETERS_DEGRADED`` on regression. ``auto_demote=True`` writes a rollback ``ParameterSet`` (source=``tuner:rollback``) + companion ``PARAMS_UPDATED`` with ``reverted_from`` set; sweep skips rollbacks so they can't be demoted themselves. Signal-only by default, matching 2.4's posture.

**4.4 Blob-store GC.** ``BlobStore.put(..., expires_at=...)`` persists TTL under the reserved ``BLOB_EXPIRES_AT_KEY``. ``sweep_expired(before=None, *, prefix=\"\", dry_run=False, event_log=None) -> BlobGCReport`` drops blobs past TTL. Local reads TTL from ``.meta`` JSON sidecar; S3 reads from object Metadata via ``head_object``. Malformed TTLs / failed deletes count as ``errors`` — fail-soft. New ``BLOB_GC_SWEPT`` event.

**Pack quality evaluation.** ``PackBuilder(evaluator=...)`` hook runs after assembly, attaches ``QualityReport`` to ``pack.metadata[\"quality_report\"]``, emits ``PACK_QUALITY_SCORED`` joined via ``pack_id``. Ships ``CODE_GENERATION_PROFILE`` / ``DOMAIN_CONTEXT_PROFILE``; scorers are plug-in via ``Scorer`` Protocol. See ``docs/agent-guide/pack-quality-evaluation.md``.

## Reviewer notes

- ``CompactionReport`` + ``BlobGCReport`` follow the project ``TrellisModel`` convention (``extra=\"forbid\"`` inherited).
- ``_aggregate_success_rate`` runs a ``count()`` probe before the 10k scan and logs ``post_promotion_sample_truncated`` on saturation — no silent bias for high-volume components.
- SQLite ``compact_versions`` compares ``MIN/MAX(valid_to)`` as ``datetime``, not ISO strings — safe across mixed ``Z`` / ``+00:00`` formats.
- Base-class ``compact_versions`` / ``sweep_expired`` raise ``NotImplementedError`` so unrelated backends opt in incrementally.
- The two S3 blob round trips (``exists()`` then ``delete_object``) are preserved to keep the boolean return contract.

## Checklist

- [x] ``make lint`` passes (ruff clean on src + tests for the touched scope; one pre-existing ``UP038`` warning in ``rule_tuner.py`` is untouched).
- [x] ``make typecheck`` passes (mypy clean across 204 files).
- [x] ``make test`` passes (1888 passed, 7 skipped).
- [x] Added tests for every new branch (74 new unit tests across 6 test files).
- [x] Updated ``docs/agent-guide/README.md`` + new ``pack-quality-evaluation.md``.
- [ ] ``CHANGELOG.md`` — this repo doesn't carry one yet; skipped.
- [x] Honored CLAUDE.md hard rules (immutable traces, governed mutations, ``extra=\"forbid\"``, structlog only, typed public APIs).

## How to verify

\`\`\`bash
make lint
make typecheck
make test

trellis analyze pack-telemetry --format json
trellis analyze extractor-fallbacks --format json
\`\`\`

## Summary

- Closes six observable weaknesses identified in the 2026-04-20 Core Loop Audit (TODO.md §Logic Gaps). Each item is a bounded, reversible fix; none change public APIs or default behaviour.
- Signal-only and fail-soft wherever possible — POC-stage scope discipline: defer enforcement, workflow, and policy-gate follow-ups until a design partner pushes.
- Lands as one cohesive batch because the gaps were audited together, share schema and event-log surface area, and reviewers benefit from seeing the loop story end-to-end.

## Type of change

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Refactor (no behavior change)
- [ ] Docs / examples / skills only
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling

## Affected surface

- [ ] CLI (`trellis`)
- [ ] REST API (`trellis-api`)
- [ ] MCP server (`trellis-mcp`)
- [ ] Python SDK (`trellis_sdk`)
- [ ] Workers / extractors
- [x] Stores (trace / graph / vector / blob / event log)
- [x] Classification / retrieval / pack builder
- [x] Schemas (additive only — new enums + optional fields, no breaking change)

## What landed, by gap

### 1.1 — Tag drift / no reclassification path
- `ContentTags.classified_at` freshness stamp set by `MergedClassification.to_content_tags()` and `apply_noise_tags()`.
- New `src/trellis/classify/refresh.py` exposes `reclassify_item(...)` and `reclassify_stale(..., max_age_days=30)` — programmatic re-classification against stored items. Preserves prior tags when the fresh pipeline produces no signal (avoids regressions).
- New `EventType.TAGS_REFRESHED` with before/after diffs for audit.
- **Deferred:** scheduled reclassification jobs + graph-neighbour context auto-injection (both land cleanly on top once a consumer asks).

### 2.1 — Advisory suppression is terminal
- `Advisory` gains `status: AdvisoryStatus (ACTIVE | SUPPRESSED)`, `suppressed_at`, `suppression_reason`.
- `AdvisoryStore.suppress(id, reason)` / `restore(id)` — idempotent; `list()` excludes suppressed by default with `include_suppressed=True` opt-in.
- `run_advisory_fitness_loop()` soft-suppresses (no hard delete) and auto-restores above `suppress_below + _RESTORE_HYSTERESIS (0.05)`.
- New `EventType.ADVISORY_SUPPRESSED` / `ADVISORY_RESTORED` events.
- **Known limitation:** auto-restore only fires while suppressed advisories still get presentations; starved advisories need operator `restore()` or a future shadow-eval mode.

### 2.3 — JSONL ↔ EventLog divergence
- `PackFeedback` carries a ULID `feedback_id` that bridges JSONL append and `FEEDBACK_RECORDED` event.
- `record_feedback()` returns `FeedbackRecordResult` with per-sink emission status + captured error — no more silent fail-soft for in-process callers.
- EventLog emission is idempotent on replay (dedupes by `feedback_id`).
- `reconcile_feedback_log_to_event_log(log_dir, event_log)` backfills missing entries; safe to run repeatedly.

### 2.4 — Advisory fitness drift detection (this session)
- Windowed comparison: `drift_window_days: int | None = 7` on `analyze_advisory_effectiveness` / `run_advisory_fitness_loop`. When set and smaller than `days`, a second aggregation over the recent sub-window runs.
- Two stateless patterns: `REGIME_SHIFT_DECLINE` (recent success_rate dropped ≥ 0.25 vs. full window) and `LIFT_SIGN_FLIP` (opposite-sign lifts with `|recent_lift| ≥ 0.1`). Regime-shift wins tie-breaks — stronger operational signal.
- New `DriftPattern(StrEnum)` + `AdvisoryDriftAlert` model on `AdvisoryEffectivenessReport.advisories_drifting`.
- New `EventType.ADVISORY_DRIFT_DETECTED`; emitted fail-soft per alert.
- **Signal-only by design:** drift does NOT gate confidence updates or suppression. Auto-halting tuning on drift pairs with gap 2.2 (auto-rollback on post-promotion degradation) as complementary future work.

### 3.2 — Dedup by `item_id` only
- Opt-in `SemanticDedupConfig` constructor arg on `PackBuilder` (disabled by default — POC-safe).
- When configured, both `build()` and `build_sectioned()` run MinHash/LSH dedup (128 perms / 16 bands / 0.85 Jaccard threshold, entropy floor `min_shingles=5` to avoid false positives on short excerpts) after exact `item_id` dedup.
- `PACK_ASSEMBLED` telemetry gains `semantic_dedup_enabled` + `semantic_dedup_rejected` fields.

### 4.1 — Idempotency cache silent reset
- `MutationExecutor._seen_idempotency_keys`: FIFO eviction replaces unconditional `.clear()` on overflow.
- When no `event_log` attached and the cache rolls over, a warning log signals the production risk instead of silently allowing duplicate execution.

## Cross-cutting refactors (from the /simplify pass)

- `_emit_fitness_event` unifies the fail-soft emission path; `_emit_advisory_event` + `_emit_drift_event` are thin adapters.
- `_lift_vs_baseline` centralises the rate/baseline/lift math + zero-sample fallback policy across full-window scoring and drift detection.
- `_WindowAggregate` dataclass replaces a 5-tuple return from `_aggregate_advisory_outcomes`; cuts `_detect_drift_alerts` kwargs from 8 → 5.
- `DriftPattern(StrEnum)` in `schemas/advisory.py` matches existing `AdvisoryStatus` / `AdvisoryCategory` convention.

## Checklist

- [x] `ruff check` passes on touched files (one pre-existing unrelated `UP038` in `rule_tuner.py:203`, not from this PR).
- [x] `mypy src/` passes (198 source files, no issues).
- [x] `pytest tests/` passes (1766 passed, 7 skipped).
- [x] Added or updated tests for the change (unit-scoped, `tmp_path` fixtures, ~50 new tests across the six gaps).
- [x] Honoured the hard rules in `CLAUDE.md` (immutable traces, governed mutations, `extra="forbid"`, structlog only, type hints).
- [ ] Docs / examples / skills — no behaviour-visible changes. `TODO.md` entries updated with completion records + next-candidate list.
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` — not touched; happy to add if you want these rolled into a release note.

## How to verify

```bash
# Lint + typecheck + full suite
ruff check src/ tests/
mypy src/
pytest tests/ -q

# Gap-by-gap focused runs
pytest tests/unit/classify/test_refresh.py -v                    # 1.1
pytest tests/unit/stores/test_advisory_store.py -v               # 2.1
pytest tests/unit/feedback/ -v                                   # 2.3
pytest tests/unit/retrieve/test_effectiveness.py::TestAdvisoryDriftDetection -v  # 2.4
pytest tests/unit/retrieve/test_pack_builder.py -v               # 3.2
pytest tests/unit/mutate/test_executor.py -v                     # 4.1
```

## Linked issues

None — the Logic Gaps live in `TODO.md §Logic Gaps — Core Loop Audit (2026-04-20)`. Next candidates flagged there: **4.3** (extractor tier fallback telemetry), **3.1** (token-budget boundary accuracy), **4.2** (temporal graph compaction), **3.4** (close loop on rejection telemetry).

## Summary

- Rewrite README lede to surface actual differentiators: **feedback attributed to exact items**, **governed parameter promotion**, and **LLM bootstraps, deterministic inherits**. Adds a new "How Trellis improves" section above the feedback-loop diagram so the thesis isn't buried in architecture details.
- Reposition backends: **pgvector is the blessed cloud default** (collocates keyword + semantic + graph in one transactional store), **SQLite stays the local default**, **LanceDB drops to alternate**, **SurrealDB is called out as coming next**. Updates substrates diagram, storage table, YAML config example, architecture + feedback-loop diagrams, and install extras to match.
- TODO.md: two new explorations surfaced during the messaging review — flowing promoted precedents into main packs (gated on baseline usefulness), and call-time strategy weights with effectiveness attribution.

body:
## Summary

Ships the end-to-end loop that lets Trellis tune its own retrieval / reranker / advisory / scoring knobs based on observed agent outcomes, with per-`(component, domain, intent_family, tool_name)` granularity and a governed promotion pipeline.

**Aligned with the recently-landed ADRs:** lives entirely in the Operational Plane (per `adr-planes-and-substrates.md`); adopts the canonical terminology from `adr-terminology.md` ("feedback loop" / "parameter tuning", not "self-learning"); `enrich` is the phase name, consistent with enrichment-mode terminology.

## What's live

### Ops tier (Operational Plane)

- **`OutcomeEvent` + `ComponentOutcome`** schemas — per-call signal with learning / identity / audit axes.
- **`OutcomeStore`** — high-volume append-only SQLite-backed store of raw outcomes.
- **`ParameterStore`** — versioned snapshots keyed by scope; 5-step precedence-chain `resolve()` (`(component, domain, intent, tool)` → back off).
- **`TunerStateStore`** — tuner proposals + per-tuner cursors.
- All three wired under `registry.operational.*` with the plane-namespaced config / DSN story.

### Runtime resolver

- **`ParameterRegistry`** — thin facade over `ParameterStore` with caching + `invalidate(scope)`. Constructed with `store=None` or missing snapshots → returns caller defaults. Every migration keeps hardcoded defaults as the fallback, so every commit is behaviour-preserving in the absence of a promoted snapshot.

### Migrated components

| Surface | Tuneable params |
|---|---|
| `retrieve.effectiveness.items` / `.advisory` | `success_rating_threshold`, `noise_rate_threshold`, `min_presentations`, `suppress_confidence`, `blend_weight` |
| `learning.scoring` | `promote_success_threshold`, `promote_retry_threshold`, `noise_success_threshold`, `noise_retry_threshold` |
| `retrieve.strategies.*` (Keyword / Semantic / Graph) | `recency_half_life_days`, `recency_floor`, `domain_match_boost`, `curated_boost`, `description_boost`, `position_decay_step` (per-`(component, domain)`) |
| `retrieve.rerankers.RRFReranker` | `k` |
| `retrieve.rerankers.MMRReranker` | `lambda_param`, `shingle_size` |

### Wiring

- MCP `_build_pack_builder` and REST `POST /api/v1/packs` construct PackBuilder with `reranker=RRFReranker()` and thread a `ParameterRegistry(registry.operational.parameter_store)` through `build_strategies` and `build_reranker`.
- `record_feedback(..., outcome_store=..., component_id=...)` dual-emits `PackFeedback → OutcomeEvent` alongside the JSONL + `FEEDBACK_RECORDED` event.

### Tuner loop

- **`RuleTuner`** — pure `aggregate_outcomes` + `apply_rules` core; `TuningRule` is data (condition op, threshold, target param/value). Three `DEFAULT_RULES` cover representative retrieval-tuning triggers. Orchestrator reads `OutcomeStore`, aggregates per cell, persists `ParameterProposal`s with deterministic ids, and advances a per-tuner cursor in `TunerStateStore`. Never overwrites terminal-status records.
- **`promote_proposal`** — governance pipeline: validate → policy gate (`min_sample_size`, `min_effect_size`, `allow_no_baseline`, `allow_non_numeric`) → merge + `ParameterStore.put` → update proposal status → invalidate `ParameterRegistry` → emit `PARAMS_UPDATED`. Policy rejection emits `TUNER_PROPOSAL_REJECTED` with a recorded reason. New `EventType`s: `PARAMS_UPDATED`, `TUNER_PROPOSAL_CREATED`, `TUNER_PROPOSAL_REJECTED`.

### Operator surface

**`trellis metrics`** — five subcommands, all support `--format json`:

- `outcomes` — cell-level aggregates filtered by `--component-id` / `--domain` / `--intent` / `--phase` / `--days`.
- `proposals` — list proposals; filter by `--tuner` / `--status`.
- `versions <component_id>` — `ParameterSet` history + active snapshot for a scope.
- `tune` — run one `RuleTuner` pass.
- `promote <proposal_id>` — dry-run by default; `--commit` to apply. `--min-sample-size` / `--min-effect-size` / `--force` override the gate.

## Aligned with ADRs on main

- **Planes:** OutcomeStore / ParameterStore / TunerStateStore live in `_BUILTIN_BACKENDS["operational"]`. SurrealDB-as-Knowledge-substrate discussion does not touch this tier (captured as a follow-up note in `TODO.md` for auditing which knobs become redundant if/when a native hybrid substrate lands).
- **Terminology:** No uses of "self-learning" in new code. The plan section in `TODO.md` is titled *"Feedback-Driven Parameter Tuning"*, explicitly noting the rename.
- **Cross-plane bridges:** `record_feedback` dual-emit honours the two sanctioned bridges (MutationExecutor and the effectiveness feedback loop); `PARAMS_UPDATED` goes through the `EventLog`, raw `OutcomeEvent`s do not.

## Commits (15)

```
571bbe4 Add trellis metrics CLI — operator surface for the tuning loop
10af449 Add promote_proposal — governed parameter promotion pipeline
9b49c90 Add RuleTuner orchestrator — cursor-driven run() with store I/O
0264373 Add RuleTuner pure core — aggregation + rule application
fcdc76f Wire ParameterRegistry into MCP + REST pack builders
5f6da2e Route RRF and MMR reranker constants through ParameterRegistry
8cd01db Route strategies.py scoring params through ParameterRegistry
9721bef Place ops stores inside Operational Plane namespace
065e22f TODO — note SurrealDB substrate tuner-knob audit
507eee3 Document Feedback-Driven Parameter Tuning plan
decbe27 Bridge PackFeedback to OutcomeEvent via record_feedback dual-emit
d2544e2 Wire RRFReranker as default + auto-enable SemanticSearch
b5c8f5b Route effectiveness and scoring thresholds through ParameterRegistry
8c05a04 Add ParameterRegistry — runtime facade over ParameterStore
d270e45 Add ops foundation — OutcomeEvent + ops stores + record_outcome
```

## Stats

- **+6.6k lines, +133 new tests** (1652 total passing, 45 skipped).
- Lint (ruff) and typecheck (mypy) clean.
- No changes to agent-facing APIs; every migration preserves the hardcoded default as the fallback when no snapshot is promoted.

## Deferred (follow-up PRs)

- Route `promote_proposal` through `MutationExecutor` (shape already matches; needs a parameter-mutation operation registration).
- SurrealDB-substrate tuner-knob audit (note captured in `TODO.md`).
- Discovery endpoint, LLM query rewrite, cross-encoder rerank, BanditTuner.

## Test plan

- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && pytest tests/unit/ -q` — currently 1652 passing, 45 skipped.
- [ ] Spot-check a pack assembly end-to-end: start `trellis-api`, assemble a pack, verify a `FEEDBACK_RECORDED` event is emitted and (with `outcome_store` passed) an `OutcomeEvent` lands.
- [ ] Run `trellis metrics outcomes --format json` on a seeded store; confirm aggregates match expected counts.
- [ ] Run `trellis metrics tune --format json` then `trellis metrics promote <id> --commit`; confirm `PARAMS_UPDATED` event, active `ParameterSet` changes, and subsequent pack builds see the new values.
